description: Brain19 Foundation Knowledge - Core ontology, categories, and relations

concepts.label: Entity
concepts.definition: The most general category; anything that exists or can be conceived.

concepts.label: Object
concepts.definition: A concrete or abstract thing that can be perceived or thought about.

concepts.label: Action
concepts.definition: A process or activity performed by an agent.

concepts.label: Property
concepts.definition: An attribute or quality of an entity.

concepts.label: Relation
concepts.definition: A connection or association between two or more entities.

concepts.label: Event
concepts.definition: Something that happens at a particular time and place.

concepts.label: State
concepts.definition: A condition or mode of being at a given time.

concepts.label: Process
concepts.definition: A series of actions or changes leading to a result.

concepts.label: Abstract
concepts.definition: Existing in thought or as an idea, not physically.

concepts.label: Concrete
concepts.definition: Having a physical, tangible existence.

concepts.label: Physical
concepts.definition: Relating to the material world and natural forces.

concepts.label: Mental
concepts.definition: Relating to the mind and cognitive processes.

concepts.label: Social
concepts.definition: Relating to society and interactions between individuals.

concepts.label: Temporal
concepts.definition: Relating to time.

concepts.label: Spatial
concepts.definition: Relating to space and position.

concepts.label: Category
concepts.definition: A class or division of entities sharing common attributes.

concepts.label: Type
concepts.definition: A subcategory or classification within a category.

concepts.label: Instance
concepts.definition: A specific example or occurrence of a type.

concepts.label: Concept
concepts.definition: An abstract idea or mental representation.

concepts.label: Definition
concepts.definition: A precise statement of the meaning of a word or concept.

concepts.label: Cause
concepts.definition: Something that produces an effect or result.

concepts.label: Effect
concepts.definition: A result produced by a cause.

concepts.label: System
concepts.definition: A set of interacting components forming a complex whole.

concepts.label: Structure
concepts.definition: The arrangement and organisation of parts within a whole.

concepts.label: Function
concepts.definition: The purpose or role something serves.

concepts.label: Pattern
concepts.definition: A recurring arrangement or sequence.

concepts.label: Change
concepts.definition: The act or instance of becoming different.

concepts.label: Quantity
concepts.definition: An amount or number of something.

concepts.label: Quality
concepts.definition: A characteristic or attribute of something.

concepts.label: Value
concepts.definition: The worth, importance, or usefulness of something.

concepts.label: Truth
concepts.definition: Correspondence with fact or reality.

concepts.label: Knowledge
concepts.definition: Justified true belief; information held with confidence.

concepts.label: Information
concepts.definition: Data that has been processed into meaningful form.

concepts.label: Meaning
concepts.definition: The significance or sense conveyed by something.

concepts.label: Evidence
concepts.definition: Facts or observations supporting a conclusion.

concepts.label: Rule
concepts.definition: A prescribed directive governing behaviour or structure.

concepts.label: Law
concepts.definition: A fundamental principle governing a domain.

concepts.label: Principle
concepts.definition: A fundamental truth or proposition serving as a foundation.

concepts.label: Model
concepts.definition: A simplified representation of a system or phenomenon.

concepts.label: Theory
concepts.definition: A well-substantiated explanation of some aspect of the natural world.

concepts.label: Hypothesis
concepts.definition: A proposed explanation made on the basis of limited evidence.

concepts.label: Fact
concepts.definition: Something known to be true based on evidence.

concepts.label: Measurement
concepts.definition: The assignment of a numerical value to a property.

concepts.label: Unit
concepts.definition: A standard quantity used to express a measurement.

concepts.label: Dimension
concepts.definition: A measurable extent such as length, width, or time.

concepts.label: Boundary
concepts.definition: A limit or edge of something.

concepts.label: Context
concepts.definition: The circumstances surrounding an event or statement.

concepts.label: Scope
concepts.definition: The extent or range of something.

concepts.label: Identity
concepts.definition: The set of qualities that make something what it is.

concepts.label: Existence
concepts.definition: The state of having objective reality.

concepts.label: Organism
concepts.definition: A living individual capable of growth and reproduction.

concepts.label: Person
concepts.definition: A human being.

concepts.label: Animal
concepts.definition: A multicellular organism that feeds on organic matter.

concepts.label: Plant
concepts.definition: A photosynthetic multicellular organism.

concepts.label: Microorganism
concepts.definition: An organism too small to see with the naked eye.

concepts.label: Place
concepts.definition: A particular position or area.

concepts.label: Country
concepts.definition: A sovereign political territory.

concepts.label: City
concepts.definition: A large settlement of people.

concepts.label: Region
concepts.definition: An area with common characteristics.

concepts.label: Continent
concepts.definition: One of the large landmasses on Earth.

concepts.label: Ocean
concepts.definition: A vast body of salt water covering most of Earth.

concepts.label: Mountain
concepts.definition: A large natural elevation of the Earth's surface.

concepts.label: River
concepts.definition: A large natural stream of water flowing to the sea.

concepts.label: Science
concepts.definition: Systematic study of the natural world through observation and experiment.

concepts.label: Physics
concepts.definition: The science of matter, energy, and their interactions.

concepts.label: Chemistry
concepts.definition: The science of substances, their properties, and reactions.

concepts.label: Biology
concepts.definition: The science of living organisms.

concepts.label: Mathematics
concepts.definition: The abstract science of number, quantity, and space.

concepts.label: Astronomy
concepts.definition: The science of celestial objects and the universe.

concepts.label: Geology
concepts.definition: The science of the Earth's physical structure.

concepts.label: Ecology
concepts.definition: The study of organisms and their environment.

concepts.label: Medicine
concepts.definition: The science and practice of diagnosing and treating disease.

concepts.label: Psychology
concepts.definition: The scientific study of the mind and behaviour.

concepts.label: Economics
concepts.definition: The study of production, distribution, and consumption of goods.

concepts.label: Sociology
concepts.definition: The study of human social behaviour and institutions.

concepts.label: Philosophy
concepts.definition: The study of fundamental questions about existence, knowledge, and values.

concepts.label: Language
concepts.definition: A structured system of communication.

concepts.label: Art
concepts.definition: Creative expression of ideas and emotions.

concepts.label: Music
concepts.definition: Organised sound in time.

concepts.label: Literature
concepts.definition: Written works of artistic merit.

concepts.label: History
concepts.definition: The study and record of past events.

concepts.label: Technology
concepts.definition: The application of scientific knowledge for practical purposes.

concepts.label: Computer
concepts.definition: An electronic device for processing data.

concepts.label: Software
concepts.definition: Programs and data that run on a computer.

concepts.label: Hardware
concepts.definition: The physical components of a computer system.

concepts.label: Algorithm
concepts.definition: A step-by-step procedure for solving a problem.

concepts.label: Network
concepts.definition: A system of interconnected nodes or devices.

concepts.label: Internet
concepts.definition: A global system of interconnected computer networks.

concepts.label: Database
concepts.definition: An organised collection of structured data.

concepts.label: Society
concepts.definition: A group of individuals sharing a common culture.

concepts.label: Government
concepts.definition: The system by which a state is governed.

concepts.label: Culture
concepts.definition: The ideas, customs, and social behaviour of a group.

concepts.label: Education
concepts.definition: The process of receiving or giving systematic instruction.

concepts.label: Religion
concepts.definition: A system of faith and worship.

concepts.label: Weather
concepts.definition: The state of the atmosphere at a place and time.

concepts.label: Climate
concepts.definition: Long-term average weather patterns in a region.

concepts.label: Ecosystem
concepts.definition: A community of organisms and their physical environment.

concepts.label: Time
concepts.definition: The indefinite continued progress of existence.

concepts.label: Space
concepts.definition: The boundless extent in which objects exist.

concepts.label: Matter
concepts.definition: Anything that has mass and occupies space.

concepts.label: Energy
concepts.definition: The capacity to do work.

concepts.label: Food
concepts.definition: Substances consumed for nutrition.

concepts.label: Water
concepts.definition: A chemical compound (H2O) essential for life.

concepts.label: Air
concepts.definition: The mixture of gases surrounding the Earth.

concepts.label: Earth
concepts.definition: The planet on which we live.

concepts.label: Sun
concepts.definition: The star at the centre of our solar system.

concepts.label: Moon
concepts.definition: A natural satellite orbiting a planet.

concepts.label: Star
concepts.definition: A luminous celestial body of plasma held together by gravity.

concepts.label: Planet
concepts.definition: A celestial body orbiting a star, massive enough to be round.

concepts.label: Galaxy
concepts.definition: A massive system of stars, gas, and dust bound by gravity.

concepts.label: Universe
concepts.definition: All of space, time, matter, and energy.

concepts.label: Gravity
concepts.definition: The force of attraction between masses.

concepts.label: Light
concepts.definition: Electromagnetic radiation visible to the human eye.

concepts.label: Sound
concepts.definition: Vibrations travelling through a medium, perceived by hearing.

concepts.label: Heat
concepts.definition: Thermal energy transferred due to temperature difference.

concepts.label: Electricity
concepts.definition: The flow of electric charge.

concepts.label: Magnetism
concepts.definition: A force caused by moving electric charges.

concepts.label: Wave
concepts.definition: A disturbance that transfers energy through a medium.

concepts.label: Pressure
concepts.definition: Force per unit area.

concepts.label: Temperature
concepts.definition: A measure of the average kinetic energy of particles.

concepts.label: Speed
concepts.definition: The rate of change of position.

concepts.label: Acceleration
concepts.definition: The rate of change of velocity.

concepts.label: Force
concepts.definition: An interaction that changes the motion of an object.

concepts.label: Mass
concepts.definition: A measure of the amount of matter in an object.

concepts.label: Volume
concepts.definition: The amount of three-dimensional space occupied by an object.

concepts.label: Density
concepts.definition: Mass per unit volume.

concepts.label: Velocity
concepts.definition: Speed in a given direction.

concepts.label: Momentum
concepts.definition: The product of mass and velocity.

concepts.label: Frequency
concepts.definition: The number of occurrences of a repeating event per unit time.

concepts.label: Wavelength
concepts.definition: The distance between successive crests of a wave.

concepts.label: Amplitude
concepts.definition: The maximum displacement of a wave from its rest position.

concepts.label: Color
concepts.definition: The visual perception of different wavelengths of light.

concepts.label: Emotion
concepts.definition: A complex psychological state involving subjective experience.

concepts.label: Consciousness
concepts.definition: Awareness of internal and external existence.

concepts.label: Intelligence
concepts.definition: The ability to acquire and apply knowledge and skills.

concepts.label: Memory_Faculty
concepts.definition: The cognitive faculty of encoding, storing, and retrieving information.

concepts.label: Perception
concepts.definition: The organisation and interpretation of sensory information.

concepts.label: Reasoning
concepts.definition: The process of drawing conclusions from evidence.

concepts.label: Logic
concepts.definition: The systematic study of valid inference.

concepts.label: Probability
concepts.definition: A measure of the likelihood that an event will occur.

concepts.label: Statistics
concepts.definition: The collection, analysis, and interpretation of numerical data.

concepts.label: Experiment
concepts.definition: A procedure carried out to test a hypothesis.

concepts.label: Observation
concepts.definition: The act of perceiving and recording phenomena.

concepts.label: Data
concepts.definition: Facts and statistics collected for analysis.

concepts.label: Communication
concepts.definition: The exchange of information between entities.

concepts.label: Symbol
concepts.definition: Something that represents or stands for something else.

concepts.label: Number
concepts.definition: A mathematical object used to count, measure, and label.

concepts.label: Set
concepts.definition: A well-defined collection of distinct objects.

concepts.label: Atom
concepts.definition: The smallest unit of a chemical element.

concepts.label: Element
concepts.definition: A substance consisting of atoms with the same atomic number.

concepts.label: Molecule
concepts.definition: Two or more atoms bonded together.

concepts.label: Compound
concepts.definition: A substance formed from two or more elements chemically bonded.

concepts.label: Ion
concepts.definition: An atom or molecule with a net electric charge.

concepts.label: Electron
concepts.definition: A subatomic particle with a negative charge.

concepts.label: Proton
concepts.definition: A subatomic particle with a positive charge.

concepts.label: Neutron
concepts.definition: A subatomic particle with no charge.

concepts.label: Nucleus
concepts.definition: The dense central region of an atom.

concepts.label: Chemical_Bond
concepts.definition: An attraction between atoms allowing formation of compounds.

concepts.label: Reaction
concepts.definition: A process in which substances are transformed.

concepts.label: Catalyst
concepts.definition: A substance that increases reaction rate without being consumed.

concepts.label: Solution
concepts.definition: A homogeneous mixture of two or more substances.

concepts.label: Acid
concepts.definition: A substance that donates protons or accepts electron pairs.

concepts.label: Base
concepts.definition: A substance that accepts protons or donates electron pairs.

concepts.label: Oxidation
concepts.definition: Loss of electrons in a chemical reaction.

concepts.label: Reduction
concepts.definition: Gain of electrons in a chemical reaction.

concepts.label: Entropy
concepts.definition: A measure of disorder or randomness in a system.

concepts.label: Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: The branch of physics dealing with heat and energy transfer.

concepts.label: Electromagnetism
concepts.definition: The interaction of electric and magnetic fields.

concepts.label: Quantum_Mechanics
concepts.definition: The branch of physics dealing with subatomic phenomena.

concepts.label: Relativity
concepts.definition: Einstein's theories relating space, time, and gravity.

concepts.label: Field
concepts.definition: A region in which a force or effect is exerted.

concepts.label: Particle
concepts.definition: A minute portion of matter.

concepts.label: Photon
concepts.definition: A quantum of electromagnetic radiation.

concepts.label: Spectrum
concepts.definition: The range of electromagnetic radiation by wavelength.

concepts.label: Cell
concepts.definition: The basic structural and functional unit of life.

concepts.label: DNA
concepts.definition: Deoxyribonucleic acid; carries genetic instructions.

concepts.label: RNA
concepts.definition: Ribonucleic acid; involved in protein synthesis.

concepts.label: Gene
concepts.definition: A unit of heredity encoding a functional product.

concepts.label: Chromosome
concepts.definition: A structure of DNA and protein carrying genes.

concepts.label: Protein
concepts.definition: A macromolecule composed of amino acid chains.

concepts.label: Enzyme
concepts.definition: A protein that catalyses biochemical reactions.

concepts.label: Metabolism
concepts.definition: The set of chemical reactions sustaining life.

concepts.label: Photosynthesis
concepts.definition: Conversion of light energy into chemical energy by organisms.

concepts.label: Respiration
concepts.definition: The metabolic process of releasing energy from nutrients.

concepts.label: Evolution
concepts.definition: Change in heritable characteristics of populations over generations.

concepts.label: Natural_Selection
concepts.definition: Differential survival and reproduction of organisms.

concepts.label: Species
concepts.definition: A group of organisms capable of interbreeding.

concepts.label: Mutation
concepts.definition: A change in the DNA sequence of an organism.

concepts.label: Heredity
concepts.definition: The passing of traits from parents to offspring.

concepts.label: Virus
concepts.definition: A submicroscopic infectious agent that replicates inside cells.

concepts.label: Bacteria
concepts.definition: Single-celled prokaryotic microorganisms.

concepts.label: Tissue
concepts.definition: A group of cells with a similar structure and function.

concepts.label: Organ
concepts.definition: A structure composed of tissues performing a specific function.

concepts.label: Nervous_System
concepts.definition: The network of nerve cells that coordinates actions.

concepts.label: Immune_System
concepts.definition: The biological defence system against pathogens.

concepts.label: Brain
concepts.definition: The organ that serves as the centre of the nervous system.

concepts.label: Neuron
concepts.definition: A nerve cell that transmits electrical signals.

concepts.label: Integer
concepts.definition: A whole number, positive, negative, or zero.

concepts.label: Real_Number
concepts.definition: A number on the continuous number line.

concepts.label: Complex_Number
concepts.definition: A number with a real and an imaginary part.

concepts.label: Variable
concepts.definition: A symbol representing an unknown or changeable value.

concepts.label: Equation
concepts.definition: A statement asserting the equality of two expressions.

concepts.label: Inequality
concepts.definition: A relation expressing that one quantity is less or greater.

concepts.label: Math_Function
concepts.definition: A mapping from inputs to outputs.

concepts.label: Limit
concepts.definition: The value a function approaches as input approaches a point.

concepts.label: Derivative
concepts.definition: The rate of change of a function with respect to a variable.

concepts.label: Integral
concepts.definition: The accumulation of quantities over a range.

concepts.label: Proof
concepts.definition: A logical argument establishing the truth of a theorem.

concepts.label: Theorem
concepts.definition: A statement proven on the basis of axioms.

concepts.label: Axiom
concepts.definition: A statement accepted as true without proof.

concepts.label: Algebra
concepts.definition: The branch of mathematics dealing with symbols and rules.

concepts.label: Geometry
concepts.definition: The branch of mathematics dealing with shapes and space.

concepts.label: Calculus
concepts.definition: The branch of mathematics studying continuous change.

concepts.label: Topology
concepts.definition: The study of properties preserved under continuous deformation.

concepts.label: Graph
concepts.definition: A set of vertices connected by edges.

concepts.label: Matrix
concepts.definition: A rectangular array of numbers.

concepts.label: Vector
concepts.definition: A quantity with both magnitude and direction.

concepts.label: Scalar
concepts.definition: A quantity with only magnitude.

concepts.label: Infinity
concepts.definition: A concept of something without bound.

concepts.label: Zero
concepts.definition: The integer denoting no quantity; the additive identity.

concepts.label: Pi
concepts.definition: The ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter.

concepts.label: Turing_Machine
concepts.definition: An abstract model of computation.

concepts.label: Complexity
concepts.definition: A measure of resources required to solve a problem.

concepts.label: Recursion
concepts.definition: A process that calls itself as part of its definition.

concepts.label: Boolean
concepts.definition: A data type with two values: true and false.

concepts.label: Bit
concepts.definition: The smallest unit of digital information (0 or 1).

concepts.label: Byte
concepts.definition: A unit of digital information equal to 8 bits.

concepts.label: Encryption
concepts.definition: The process of encoding information for security.

concepts.label: Operating_System
concepts.definition: Software that manages computer hardware and software resources.

concepts.label: Programming_Language
concepts.definition: A formal language for writing computer programs.

concepts.label: Artificial_Intelligence
concepts.definition: The simulation of intelligent behaviour by machines.

concepts.label: Machine_Learning
concepts.definition: A subset of AI where systems learn from data.

concepts.label: Neural_Network
concepts.definition: A computing model inspired by biological neural networks.

concepts.label: Walking
concepts.definition: Moving on foot at a moderate pace by alternately setting each foot forward

concepts.label: Running
concepts.definition: Moving rapidly on foot with both feet temporarily leaving the ground during each stride

concepts.label: Sprinting
concepts.definition: Running at maximum speed over a short distance

concepts.label: Jogging
concepts.definition: Running at a slow, steady pace as a form of aerobic exercise

concepts.label: Jumping
concepts.definition: Propelling oneself upward or forward by a sudden muscular effort

concepts.label: Leaping
concepts.definition: Jumping with a long, bounding motion

concepts.label: Hopping
concepts.definition: Moving by jumping on one foot or with both feet together

concepts.label: Climbing
concepts.definition: Moving up, down, or across something using hands and feet

concepts.label: Crawling
concepts.definition: Moving on hands and knees or by dragging the body along the ground

concepts.label: Creeping
concepts.definition: Moving slowly and carefully, especially on hands and knees

concepts.label: Swimming
concepts.definition: Moving through water by coordinated movement of the limbs

concepts.label: Diving
concepts.definition: Plunging head first into water or descending underwater

concepts.label: Floating
concepts.definition: Remaining suspended in water or air without sinking

concepts.label: Flying
concepts.definition: Moving through the air under one's own power or in an aircraft

concepts.label: Gliding
concepts.definition: Moving smoothly and continuously through air or over a surface

concepts.label: Soaring
concepts.definition: Flying at a high altitude without flapping wings or using engine power

concepts.label: Driving
concepts.definition: Operating and controlling the direction and speed of a motor vehicle

concepts.label: Riding
concepts.definition: Sitting on and controlling the movement of an animal or vehicle

concepts.label: Cycling
concepts.definition: Riding a bicycle by pedaling

concepts.label: Sailing
concepts.definition: Traveling in a boat using wind power captured by sails

concepts.label: Rowing
concepts.definition: Propelling a boat through water using oars

concepts.label: Paddling
concepts.definition: Propelling a boat using a paddle with broad blade

concepts.label: Skating
concepts.definition: Moving smoothly over a surface using skates

concepts.label: Skiing
concepts.definition: Moving over snow on skis, typically as a sport or recreation

concepts.label: Snowboarding
concepts.definition: Sliding downhill on snow while standing on a snowboard

concepts.label: Skateboarding
concepts.definition: Riding and performing tricks on a skateboard

concepts.label: Surfing
concepts.definition: Riding waves on a surfboard

concepts.label: Eating
concepts.definition: Consuming food by chewing and swallowing

concepts.label: Drinking
concepts.definition: Taking liquid into the mouth and swallowing it

concepts.label: Chewing
concepts.definition: Working food in the mouth with the teeth

concepts.label: Swallowing
concepts.definition: Causing food or drink to pass down the throat

concepts.label: Tasting
concepts.definition: Perceiving the flavor of food or drink

concepts.label: Sipping
concepts.definition: Drinking in small quantities

concepts.label: Gulping
concepts.definition: Swallowing hastily or greedily

concepts.label: Sleeping
concepts.definition: A naturally recurring state of mind and body rest

concepts.label: Dreaming
concepts.definition: Experiencing sequences of images and emotions during sleep

concepts.label: Waking
concepts.definition: Emerging from sleep

concepts.label: Napping
concepts.definition: Taking a short sleep during the day

concepts.label: Resting
concepts.definition: Ceasing work or movement in order to relax or recover

concepts.label: Relaxing
concepts.definition: Making or becoming less tense or anxious

concepts.label: Washing
concepts.definition: Cleaning with water and typically soap

concepts.label: Bathing
concepts.definition: Washing the body in a bath or shower

concepts.label: Showering
concepts.definition: Washing under a spray of water

concepts.label: Brushing
concepts.definition: Cleaning or grooming with a brush

concepts.label: Combing
concepts.definition: Arranging hair with a comb

concepts.label: Shaving
concepts.definition: Cutting hair close to the skin with a razor

concepts.label: Cooking
concepts.definition: Preparing food by heating and combining ingredients

concepts.label: Baking
concepts.definition: Cooking food by dry heat in an oven

concepts.label: Frying
concepts.definition: Cooking food in hot fat or oil

concepts.label: Boiling
concepts.definition: Cooking in water at 100 degrees Celsius

concepts.label: Steaming
concepts.definition: Cooking with the steam from boiling water

concepts.label: Grilling
concepts.definition: Cooking food on a metal framework over fire

concepts.label: Roasting
concepts.definition: Cooking food with dry heat in an oven or over fire

concepts.label: Cleaning
concepts.definition: Making something free of dirt, marks, or mess

concepts.label: Sweeping
concepts.definition: Cleaning a floor with a broom

concepts.label: Mopping
concepts.definition: Cleaning a floor with a wet mop

concepts.label: Vacuuming
concepts.definition: Cleaning with a vacuum cleaner

concepts.label: Dusting
concepts.definition: Removing dust from surfaces

concepts.label: Wiping
concepts.definition: Cleaning by rubbing with a cloth or similar

concepts.label: Scrubbing
concepts.definition: Cleaning by rubbing hard with a brush

concepts.label: Organizing
concepts.definition: Arranging items in a systematic way

concepts.label: Sorting
concepts.definition: Arranging items according to type or characteristics

concepts.label: Shopping
concepts.definition: Buying goods from stores

concepts.label: Purchasing
concepts.definition: Buying something by paying for it

concepts.label: Selecting
concepts.definition: Choosing from a number of alternatives

concepts.label: Working
concepts.definition: Engaging in physical or mental activity to achieve a result

concepts.label: Laboring
concepts.definition: Working hard, especially physical work

concepts.label: Studying
concepts.definition: Acquiring knowledge through reading and research

concepts.label: Learning
concepts.definition: Acquiring new knowledge or skills

concepts.label: Teaching
concepts.definition: Imparting knowledge or instructing

concepts.label: Playing
concepts.definition: Engaging in activity for enjoyment and recreation

concepts.label: Gaming
concepts.definition: Playing games, especially video games

concepts.label: Exercising
concepts.definition: Engaging in physical activity to improve health

concepts.label: Reading
concepts.definition: Looking at and understanding written words

concepts.label: Writing
concepts.definition: Marking letters, words, or symbols on a surface

concepts.label: Typing
concepts.definition: Writing using a keyboard

concepts.label: Drawing
concepts.definition: Creating pictures with pencil, pen, or similar tools

concepts.label: Painting
concepts.definition: Creating pictures using paint and brushes

concepts.label: Sketching
concepts.definition: Drawing quickly and roughly

concepts.label: Speaking
concepts.definition: Saying words aloud to communicate

concepts.label: Talking
concepts.definition: Speaking to communicate or express thoughts

concepts.label: Conversing
concepts.definition: Engaging in spoken exchange with others

concepts.label: Whispering
concepts.definition: Speaking very softly using breath without vocal cords

concepts.label: Shouting
concepts.definition: Speaking or calling out very loudly

concepts.label: Listening
concepts.definition: Paying attention to sound or what someone is saying

concepts.label: Hearing
concepts.definition: Perceiving sound through the ears

concepts.label: Singing
concepts.definition: Making musical sounds with the voice

concepts.label: Humming

concepts.label: Whistling

concepts.label: Dancing
concepts.definition: Moving rhythmically to music

concepts.label: Crafting
concepts.definition: Making something skillfully by hand

concepts.label: Building
concepts.definition: Constructing something by assembling parts or materials

concepts.label: Repairing
concepts.definition: Fixing something that is damaged or broken

concepts.label: Fixing
concepts.definition: Restoring something to working order

concepts.label: Assembling
concepts.definition: Putting together parts to create a whole

concepts.label: Greeting

concepts.label: Waving

concepts.label: Shaking
concepts.definition: Grasping another person's hand in greeting

concepts.label: Nodding

concepts.label: Symbiosis

concepts.label: Parasitism

concepts.label: Carrying Capacity

concepts.label: Ribosome

concepts.label: Ketone
concepts.definition: Organic compound with carbonyl group bonded to two carbon atoms

concepts.label: Ester
concepts.definition: Organic compound formed from acid and alcohol with elimination of water

concepts.label: Amine
concepts.definition: Organic compound derived from ammonia with one or more alkyl groups

concepts.label: Benzene
concepts.definition: Cyclic aromatic hydrocarbon C6H6 with delocalized pi electrons

concepts.label: Carboxylic Acid
concepts.definition: Organic acid containing carboxyl group (-COOH)

concepts.label: Phenol
concepts.definition: Aromatic compound with hydroxyl group directly bonded to benzene ring

concepts.label: Ether
concepts.definition: Organic compound with oxygen atom connected to two alkyl groups

concepts.label: Amino Acid
concepts.definition: Organic molecule containing both amino and carboxyl groups, building blocks of proteins

concepts.label: Lipid
concepts.definition: Hydrophobic organic molecule including fats, oils, and cell membrane components

concepts.label: Carbohydrate
concepts.definition: Organic molecule composed of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen, primary energy source

concepts.label: ATP
concepts.definition: Adenosine triphosphate, primary energy currency molecule of living cells

concepts.label: Glucose
concepts.definition: Simple sugar C6H12O6, primary source of energy for cellular metabolism

concepts.label: Insulin
concepts.definition: Peptide hormone regulating blood glucose levels produced by pancreas

concepts.label: Hemoglobin
concepts.definition: Iron-containing protein in red blood cells that transports oxygen

concepts.label: Electrolysis
concepts.definition: Chemical decomposition produced by passing electric current through a liquid

concepts.label: Galvanic Cell
concepts.definition: Electrochemical cell that generates electrical energy from spontaneous redox reactions

concepts.label: Electrode
concepts.definition: Electrical conductor through which current enters or leaves an electrolyte

concepts.label: Faraday Constant
concepts.definition: Electric charge per mole of electrons, approximately 96485 C/mol

concepts.label: Graphene
concepts.definition: Single layer of carbon atoms in hexagonal lattice, extremely strong and conductive

concepts.label: Carbon Nanotube
concepts.definition: Cylindrical nanostructure of rolled graphene with extraordinary properties

concepts.label: Polymerization
concepts.definition: Chemical process of combining monomers to form a polymer chain

concepts.label: Monomer
concepts.definition: Small molecule that can bond to other identical molecules to form a polymer

concepts.label: Chirality
concepts.definition: Property of a molecule that is non-superimposable on its mirror image

concepts.label: Enantiomer
concepts.definition: One of two stereoisomers that are mirror images of each other

concepts.label: Drug Receptor
concepts.definition: Protein molecule that receives chemical signals from outside the cell

concepts.label: Pharmacokinetics
concepts.definition: Study of how the body absorbs, distributes, metabolizes, and excretes drugs

concepts.label: Chromatography
concepts.definition: Technique for separating mixtures based on differential partitioning between phases

concepts.label: Mass Spectrometry
concepts.definition: Analytical technique measuring mass-to-charge ratio of ions to identify molecules

concepts.label: Titration
concepts.definition: Quantitative chemical analysis to determine concentration of a known reactant

concepts.label: Activation Energy
concepts.definition: Minimum energy required for a chemical reaction to proceed

concepts.label: Gibbs Free Energy
concepts.definition: Thermodynamic potential measuring maximum reversible work at constant T and P

concepts.label: Exothermic Reaction
concepts.definition: Chemical reaction that releases energy in the form of heat

concepts.label: Endothermic Reaction
concepts.definition: Chemical reaction that absorbs energy from the surroundings

concepts.label: Ozone
concepts.definition: Triatomic oxygen O3, forms protective layer in stratosphere

concepts.label: Greenhouse Gas
concepts.definition: Gas that absorbs and emits infrared radiation, contributing to greenhouse effect

concepts.label: Noble Gas
concepts.definition: Chemically inert elements in Group 18 with full valence electron shells

concepts.label: Transition Metal
concepts.definition: Element with partially filled d-orbitals, often forming colored compounds

concepts.label: Alloy
concepts.definition: Mixture of metals or metal with other elements with enhanced properties

concepts.label: Spectroscopy
concepts.definition: Study of interaction between matter and electromagnetic radiation

concepts.label: Chemical Equilibrium
concepts.definition: State where forward and reverse reaction rates are equal

concepts.label: Array
concepts.definition: A data structure consisting of elements stored in contiguous memory locations, accessible by index

concepts.label: CPU
concepts.definition: Central Processing Unit - the primary component that executes instructions and performs calculations

concepts.label: Memory
concepts.definition: Computer storage that holds data and instructions temporarily or permanently for processing

concepts.label: Thread
concepts.definition: A lightweight unit of execution within a process, allowing concurrent execution of code

concepts.label: Neural Network
concepts.definition: A computational model inspired by biological neural networks, used for machine learning tasks

concepts.label: Protocol
concepts.definition: A set of rules and standards that define how data is transmitted and received over a network

concepts.label: Compiler
concepts.definition: A program that translates source code written in a high-level language into machine code

concepts.label: Operating System
concepts.definition: System software that manages computer hardware and provides services for computer programs

concepts.label: API
concepts.definition: Application Programming Interface - a set of protocols and tools for building software applications

concepts.label: Hash Function
concepts.definition: A function that maps data of arbitrary size to fixed-size values, used in data structures and cryptography

concepts.label: Binary Search
concepts.definition: An efficient search algorithm that finds target values in sorted arrays by repeatedly halving the search space

concepts.label: Machine Learning
concepts.definition: A subset of AI that enables computers to learn and make decisions from data without explicit programming

concepts.label: Turing Machine
concepts.definition: An abstract computational model that defines what it means for a function to be computable

concepts.label: TCP
concepts.definition: Transmission Control Protocol - a reliable, connection-oriented transport protocol that ensures data delivery

concepts.label: IP
concepts.definition: Internet Protocol - a network layer protocol that routes data packets between devices across networks

concepts.label: HTTP
concepts.definition: HyperText Transfer Protocol - an application layer protocol for transferring web content between clients and servers

concepts.label: DNS
concepts.definition: Domain Name System - a hierarchical naming system that translates domain names into IP addresses

concepts.label: Firewall
concepts.definition: A network security device that monitors and controls incoming and outgoing network traffic based on security rules

concepts.label: Router
concepts.definition: A network device that forwards data packets between computer networks using routing tables

concepts.label: Packet
concepts.definition: A formatted unit of data carried over a network, containing both payload and control information

concepts.label: Latency
concepts.definition: The time delay between the initiation of a network request and the beginning of a response

concepts.label: Kernel
concepts.definition: The core component of an operating system that manages system resources and provides low-level services

concepts.label: Virtual Memory
concepts.definition: A memory management technique that provides an abstraction of storage resources, allowing programs to use more memory than physically available

concepts.label: File System
concepts.definition: A method for organizing, storing, and retrieving files and directories on storage devices

concepts.label: Scheduling
concepts.definition: The process by which an operating system decides which processes or threads to execute and in what order

concepts.label: Deadlock
concepts.definition: A situation where two or more processes are blocked forever, each waiting for the other to release resources

concepts.label: SQL
concepts.definition: Structured Query Language - a standardized language for managing and querying relational databases

concepts.label: NoSQL
concepts.definition: Non-relational database systems designed for handling large volumes of unstructured or semi-structured data

concepts.label: Index
concepts.definition: A database structure that improves the speed of data retrieval operations on a table at the cost of additional storage

concepts.label: Transaction
concepts.definition: A unit of work performed within a database management system that is treated as a single logical operation

concepts.label: ACID
concepts.definition: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability - properties that guarantee reliable database transaction processing

concepts.label: Normalization
concepts.definition: The process of organizing database tables to reduce redundancy and improve data integrity

concepts.label: Gradient Descent
concepts.definition: An optimization algorithm that iteratively adjusts parameters to minimize a cost function by following the gradient

concepts.label: Backpropagation
concepts.definition: A supervised learning algorithm that trains neural networks by propagating errors backward through the network layers

concepts.label: CNN
concepts.definition: Convolutional Neural Network - a deep learning architecture particularly effective for image recognition and computer vision tasks

concepts.label: RNN
concepts.definition: Recurrent Neural Network - a neural network architecture designed to process sequential data with memory capabilities

concepts.label: Transformer
concepts.definition: A neural network architecture that uses attention mechanisms to process sequences in parallel, revolutionizing NLP

concepts.label: Reinforcement Learning
concepts.definition: A machine learning paradigm where agents learn to make decisions through trial and error interactions with an environment

concepts.label: GAN
concepts.definition: Generative Adversarial Network - two neural networks competing against each other to generate realistic synthetic data

concepts.label: Design Pattern
concepts.definition: Reusable solutions to commonly occurring problems in software design and architecture

concepts.label: Version Control
concepts.definition: A system that tracks changes to files over time, enabling collaboration and history management in software development

concepts.label: Testing
concepts.definition: The process of evaluating software to find bugs and verify that it meets specified requirements

concepts.label: CI/CD
concepts.definition: Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment - practices that enable frequent code integration and automated deployment

concepts.label: Microservice
concepts.definition: An architectural approach that structures applications as collections of loosely coupled, independently deployable services

concepts.label: REST
concepts.definition: Representational State Transfer - an architectural style for designing networked applications using HTTP methods

concepts.label: Docker
concepts.definition: A containerization platform that packages applications and their dependencies into portable, lightweight containers

concepts.label: Load Balancer
concepts.definition: A device or software that distributes network traffic across multiple servers to ensure high availability and performance

concepts.label: Cache
concepts.definition: A high-speed storage layer that stores frequently accessed data to reduce access time and improve performance

concepts.label: Mutex
concepts.definition: Mutual Exclusion - a synchronization primitive that prevents multiple threads from accessing shared resources simultaneously

concepts.label: Big O Notation
concepts.definition: A mathematical notation that describes the limiting behavior of functions, used to analyze algorithm complexity

concepts.label: Blockchain
concepts.definition: A distributed ledger technology that maintains a continuously growing list of cryptographically linked records

concepts.label: MapReduce
concepts.definition: A programming model for processing large datasets in a distributed computing environment using parallel processing

concepts.label: Kubernetes
concepts.definition: An open-source container orchestration platform for automating deployment, scaling, and management of containerized applications

concepts.label: OAuth
concepts.definition: An open standard for access delegation that allows secure authorization without sharing credentials

concepts.label: AES
concepts.definition: Advanced Encryption Standard, symmetric block cipher using 128/192/256-bit keys

concepts.label: RSA Algorithm
concepts.definition: Public-key cryptosystem based on difficulty of factoring large primes

concepts.label: Public Key Cryptography
concepts.definition: Cryptographic system using key pairs: public for encryption, private for decryption

concepts.label: Digital Signature
concepts.definition: Mathematical scheme for verifying authenticity and integrity of digital messages

concepts.label: Zero Knowledge Proof
concepts.definition: Method to prove knowledge of a value without revealing the value itself

concepts.label: SHA-256
concepts.definition: Cryptographic hash function producing 256-bit fixed-size output from arbitrary input

concepts.label: Elliptic Curve Cryptography
concepts.definition: Public-key cryptography based on algebraic structure of elliptic curves

concepts.label: Lexer
concepts.definition: Program that converts sequence of characters into sequence of tokens

concepts.label: Parser
concepts.definition: Program that analyzes token sequence according to formal grammar rules

concepts.label: AST
concepts.definition: Abstract Syntax Tree, tree representation of syntactic structure of source code

concepts.label: Code Generation
concepts.definition: Compiler phase that transforms intermediate representation into target machine code

concepts.label: JIT Compilation
concepts.definition: Just-In-Time compilation, translating code to machine code during program execution

concepts.label: Bytecode
concepts.definition: Instruction set designed for efficient execution by a software interpreter or VM

concepts.label: Consensus Algorithm
concepts.definition: Protocol achieving agreement on a single data value among distributed processes

concepts.label: Paxos
concepts.definition: Family of protocols for solving consensus in a network of unreliable processors

concepts.label: Raft
concepts.definition: Consensus algorithm designed as a more understandable alternative to Paxos

concepts.label: CAP Theorem
concepts.definition: States that distributed system cannot simultaneously guarantee consistency, availability, and partition tolerance

concepts.label: Sharding
concepts.definition: Database partitioning that separates data across multiple machines

concepts.label: Replication
concepts.definition: Maintaining copies of data on multiple machines for fault tolerance

concepts.label: Eventual Consistency
concepts.definition: Consistency model where replicas converge to same value given enough time

concepts.label: Type System
concepts.definition: Set of rules assigning types to program constructs to prevent type errors

concepts.label: Lambda Calculus
concepts.definition: Formal system for expressing computation based on function abstraction and application

concepts.label: Garbage Collection
concepts.definition: Automatic memory management reclaiming memory no longer in use by a program

concepts.label: Closure
concepts.definition: Function that captures variables from its enclosing lexical scope

concepts.label: Monad
concepts.definition: Design pattern encapsulating computations as a series of steps with defined composition rules

concepts.label: Pattern Matching
concepts.definition: Mechanism for checking a value against a pattern and destructuring data

concepts.label: ALU
concepts.definition: Arithmetic Logic Unit, combinational circuit performing arithmetic and bitwise operations

concepts.label: Register
concepts.definition: Small fast storage location within a processor for holding data being processed

concepts.label: Cache Line
concepts.definition: Unit of data transfer between main memory and CPU cache, typically 64 bytes

concepts.label: Pipeline
concepts.definition: Technique of overlapping instruction execution stages to increase throughput

concepts.label: Branch Prediction
concepts.definition: CPU technique guessing which branch of conditional code will execute next

concepts.label: FPGA
concepts.definition: Field-Programmable Gate Array, integrated circuit designed to be configured after manufacturing

concepts.label: SIMD
concepts.definition: Single Instruction Multiple Data, parallel processing applying one operation to multiple data points

concepts.label: RISC
concepts.definition: Reduced Instruction Set Computer, processor design with simple, uniform instructions

concepts.label: CISC
concepts.definition: Complex Instruction Set Computer, processor design with many specialized instructions

concepts.label: GPU
concepts.definition: Graphics Processing Unit, specialized processor for parallel computation and rendering

concepts.label: Attention Mechanism
concepts.definition: Neural network component learning to focus on relevant parts of input

concepts.label: Embedding
concepts.definition: Dense vector representation of discrete data in continuous vector space

concepts.label: Overfitting
concepts.definition: Model learning training data noise instead of underlying pattern, poor generalization

concepts.label: Regularization
concepts.definition: Technique adding penalty to loss function to prevent overfitting

concepts.label: Loss Function
concepts.definition: Function measuring difference between predicted and actual values during training

concepts.label: Distributed Hash Table
concepts.definition: Decentralized distributed system providing lookup service similar to hash table

concepts.label: Tectonic Plates
concepts.definition: Large sections of the Earth's lithosphere that move and interact at their boundaries, causing earthquakes, volcanic activity, and mountain formation.

concepts.label: Magma
concepts.definition: Molten rock material beneath the Earth's surface that can crystallize to form igneous rocks or erupt as lava.

concepts.label: Sedimentary Rock
concepts.definition: Rock formed from the accumulation and lithification of sediment, organic matter, or chemical precipitates.

concepts.label: Igneous Rock
concepts.definition: Rock formed through the cooling and solidification of magma or lava.

concepts.label: Metamorphic Rock
concepts.definition: Rock formed by the transformation of existing rock through heat, pressure, and chemical processes without melting.

concepts.label: Mineral
concepts.definition: Naturally occurring inorganic solid with a defined chemical composition and crystal structure.

concepts.label: Fossil
concepts.definition: Preserved remains or traces of ancient organisms found in sedimentary rocks.

concepts.label: Geological Time Scale
concepts.definition: System of chronological dating that describes the timing and relationships of events in Earth's history.

concepts.label: Continental Drift
concepts.definition: Theory that continents move across the Earth's surface over geological time scales.

concepts.label: Pangaea
concepts.definition: Supercontinent that existed during the late Paleozoic and early Mesozoic eras, approximately 335-175 million years ago.

concepts.label: Atmosphere
concepts.definition: Layer of gases surrounding Earth, held in place by gravity and essential for weather and climate.

concepts.label: Jet Stream
concepts.definition: Fast-moving ribbons of air in the upper atmosphere that influence weather patterns across continents.

concepts.label: Pressure System
concepts.definition: Areas of high or low atmospheric pressure that drive wind patterns and weather changes.

concepts.label: Precipitation
concepts.definition: Water released from clouds in the form of rain, snow, sleet, or hail.

concepts.label: Ocean Current
concepts.definition: Continuous, directed movement of seawater driven by wind, density differences, and gravitational forces.

concepts.label: Tide
concepts.definition: Regular rise and fall of sea levels caused primarily by gravitational forces of the moon and sun.

concepts.label: Salinity
concepts.definition: Concentration of dissolved salts in seawater, typically measured in parts per thousand.

concepts.label: Coral Reef
concepts.definition: Underwater ecosystem built by coral polyps, typically found in warm, shallow marine waters.

concepts.label: Thermocline
concepts.definition: Layer in the ocean where temperature changes rapidly with depth, separating warm surface waters from cold deep waters.

concepts.label: Tsunami
concepts.definition: Series of ocean waves caused by underwater earthquakes, volcanic eruptions, or landslides.

concepts.label: Upwelling
concepts.definition: Oceanographic phenomenon where deep, cold, nutrient-rich water rises to the surface.

concepts.label: Greenhouse Effect
concepts.definition: Process by which certain atmospheric gases trap heat from the sun, warming Earth's surface.

concepts.label: Ice Age
concepts.definition: Period of long-term reduction in Earth's surface and atmospheric temperature, resulting in expansion of ice sheets.

concepts.label: Carbon Cycle
concepts.definition: Biogeochemical cycle describing the exchange of carbon among Earth's atmosphere, oceans, biosphere, and geosphere.

concepts.label: Albedo
concepts.definition: Measure of how much solar radiation is reflected by a surface, expressed as a percentage or decimal fraction.

concepts.label: Ocean Acidification
concepts.definition: Decrease in ocean pH caused by absorption of atmospheric carbon dioxide, affecting marine ecosystems.

concepts.label: Sea Level Rise
concepts.definition: Increase in global mean sea level due to thermal expansion of seawater and melting of land-based ice.

concepts.label: Permafrost
concepts.definition: Ground that remains frozen for two or more consecutive years, primarily found in polar regions and high altitudes.

concepts.label: Deforestation
concepts.definition: Large-scale removal of forests, typically for agriculture, urban development, or logging.

concepts.label: Water Cycle
concepts.definition: Continuous movement of water through Earth's atmosphere, land, and oceans via evaporation, condensation, and precipitation.

concepts.label: Aquifer
concepts.definition: Underground layer of permeable rock or sediment that holds and transmits groundwater.

concepts.label: Watershed
concepts.definition: Area of land where all water drains to a common outlet, such as a river, lake, or ocean.

concepts.label: Glacier
concepts.definition: Large mass of ice formed from compressed snow that moves slowly under its own weight.

concepts.label: Erosion
concepts.definition: Process by which rock and soil are worn away and transported by natural forces like water, wind, and ice.

concepts.label: Weathering
concepts.definition: Chemical, physical, and biological processes that break down rocks and minerals at Earth's surface.

concepts.label: Fault
concepts.definition: Fracture in rock along which movement has occurred, often associated with earthquake activity.

concepts.label: Mantle
concepts.definition: Layer of hot, dense rock between Earth's crust and core, responsible for plate tectonic movement.

concepts.label: Convection
concepts.definition: Heat transfer process involving the movement of fluids, driving plate tectonics and atmospheric circulation.

concepts.label: Evaporation
concepts.definition: Process by which liquid water transforms into water vapor, primarily driven by solar energy.

concepts.label: Condensation
concepts.definition: Process by which water vapor cools and transforms into liquid water droplets, forming clouds.

concepts.label: Sediment
concepts.definition: Particles of rock, minerals, and organic matter transported and deposited by water, wind, or ice.

concepts.label: Supply
concepts.definition: The quantity of goods or services that producers are willing and able to offer at various price levels during a specific time period.

concepts.label: Demand
concepts.definition: The quantity of goods or services that consumers are willing and able to purchase at various price levels during a specific time period.

concepts.label: Equilibrium Price
concepts.definition: The price at which the quantity demanded by consumers equals the quantity supplied by producers, resulting in market balance.

concepts.label: Elasticity
concepts.definition: A measure of how responsive the quantity demanded or supplied of a good is to changes in price or other economic factors.

concepts.label: Marginal Cost
concepts.definition: The additional cost incurred by producing one more unit of a good or service.

concepts.label: Utility
concepts.definition: The satisfaction or benefit that consumers derive from consuming goods or services.

concepts.label: Market Failure
concepts.definition: A situation where the free market fails to allocate resources efficiently, often requiring government intervention.

concepts.label: Monopoly
concepts.definition: A market structure where a single seller controls the entire supply of a product or service with no close substitutes.

concepts.label: Oligopoly
concepts.definition: A market structure characterized by a small number of large firms that dominate the market and may engage in strategic behavior.

concepts.label: GDP
concepts.definition: Gross Domestic Product - the total monetary value of all finished goods and services produced within a country during a specific time period.

concepts.label: Inflation
concepts.definition: The sustained increase in the general price level of goods and services in an economy over time, reducing purchasing power.

concepts.label: Unemployment
concepts.definition: The condition where people who are able and willing to work cannot find employment, typically measured as a percentage of the labor force.

concepts.label: Interest Rate
concepts.definition: The cost of borrowing money or the return earned on lending money, usually expressed as a percentage per year.

concepts.label: Fiscal Policy
concepts.definition: Government policy regarding taxation, spending, and borrowing designed to influence economic activity and achieve macroeconomic objectives.

concepts.label: Monetary Policy
concepts.definition: Central bank actions involving the management of interest rates and money supply to achieve macroeconomic objectives like price stability.

concepts.label: Central Bank
concepts.definition: A national institution responsible for monetary policy, regulating the banking system, and maintaining financial stability.

concepts.label: Trade Balance
concepts.definition: The difference between the value of a country's exports and imports of goods and services over a specific period.

concepts.label: Stock
concepts.definition: A financial instrument representing ownership shares in a corporation, entitling the holder to a proportionate claim on assets and earnings.

concepts.label: Bond
concepts.definition: A debt security representing a loan made by an investor to a borrower, typically governments or corporations, with fixed interest payments.

concepts.label: Portfolio
concepts.definition: A collection of financial investments held by an individual or institution, designed to balance risk and return.

concepts.label: Risk
concepts.definition: The uncertainty regarding the outcome of an investment or financial decision, potentially resulting in loss or lower than expected returns.

concepts.label: Diversification
concepts.definition: An investment strategy that spreads risk by allocating investments across various financial instruments, industries, or other categories.

concepts.label: Compound Interest
concepts.definition: Interest calculated on the initial principal and accumulated interest from previous periods, resulting in exponential growth over time.

concepts.label: Social Structure
concepts.definition: The organized pattern of social relationships and social institutions that together compose society and define social positions.

concepts.label: Socialization
concepts.definition: The lifelong process through which people learn the attitudes, values, and behaviors appropriate for members of a particular culture.

concepts.label: Stratification
concepts.definition: The hierarchical arrangement of individuals and groups in society based on factors like wealth, power, education, and social status.

concepts.label: Social Mobility
concepts.definition: The movement of individuals or groups between different social positions within a society's stratification system.

concepts.label: Deviance
concepts.definition: Behavior that violates social norms and expectations, potentially leading to social sanctions or stigmatization.

concepts.label: Institution
concepts.definition: Established patterns of behavior and relationships that serve important social functions and are recognized as fundamental parts of a culture.

concepts.label: Conditioning
concepts.definition: A learning process where behavior is modified through the association of stimuli with responses or consequences.

concepts.label: Reinforcement
concepts.definition: A consequence that increases the likelihood of a behavior being repeated, either through positive rewards or negative removal.

concepts.label: Cognitive Development
concepts.definition: The progressive changes in mental abilities such as thinking, reasoning, and understanding that occur throughout the lifespan.

concepts.label: Motivation
concepts.definition: The psychological processes that initiate, guide, and sustain goal-directed behavior and influence the intensity of effort.

concepts.label: Personality
concepts.definition: The relatively stable patterns of thoughts, emotions, and behaviors that distinguish individuals from one another.

concepts.label: Consumer Behavior
concepts.definition: The study of individuals, groups, or organizations and the processes they use to select, purchase, and dispose of products or services.

concepts.label: Economic Growth
concepts.definition: The increase in the production of goods and services in an economy over time, typically measured by changes in GDP.

concepts.label: Market Efficiency
concepts.definition: The degree to which market prices reflect all available information and resources are allocated to their most valued uses.

concepts.label: Social Capital
concepts.definition: The networks of relationships among people in a society that enable it to function effectively through trust and reciprocity.

concepts.label: Behavioral Economics
concepts.definition: The study of how psychological, emotional, cultural, and social factors affect economic decisions and market outcomes.

concepts.label: Game Theory
concepts.definition: Mathematical framework for analyzing strategic interactions between rational decision-makers in competitive situations.

concepts.label: Human Capital
concepts.definition: The economic value of skills, knowledge, experience, and attributes possessed by individuals that contribute to productivity.

concepts.label: Externality
concepts.definition: A cost or benefit that affects third parties who did not choose to incur that cost or benefit from an economic transaction.

concepts.label: Recession
concepts.definition: A significant decline in economic activity lasting more than a few months, typically characterized by falling GDP and rising unemployment.

concepts.label: Liquidity
concepts.definition: The ease with which an asset can be converted into cash without significantly affecting its market price.

concepts.label: Social Norm
concepts.definition: Informal rules that govern behavior in groups and societies, specifying what is acceptable and unacceptable conduct.

concepts.label: Ohm's Law
concepts.definition: A fundamental law in electrical engineering stating that the current through a conductor between two points is directly proportional to the voltage across the two points, expressed as V = I × R

concepts.label: Kirchhoff's Laws
concepts.definition: Two fundamental laws in circuit analysis: Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL) and Kirchhoff's Voltage Law (KVL), governing the conservation of charge and energy in electrical circuits

concepts.label: Capacitor
concepts.definition: A passive electronic component that stores electrical energy in an electric field, characterized by its capacitance measured in farads

concepts.label: Inductor
concepts.definition: A passive electronic component that stores energy in a magnetic field when electric current flows through it, characterized by its inductance measured in henries

concepts.label: Diode
concepts.definition: A semiconductor device that allows current to flow in only one direction, commonly used for rectification and voltage regulation

concepts.label: Amplifier
concepts.definition: An electronic device that increases the power of a signal by taking energy from a power supply and controlling the output to match the input signal but with larger amplitude

concepts.label: Oscillator
concepts.definition: An electronic circuit that produces a repetitive, oscillating electronic signal, often a sine wave or square wave, without any input signal

concepts.label: Impedance
concepts.definition: The measure of opposition that a circuit presents to a current when a voltage is applied, combining resistance and reactance in AC circuits

concepts.label: AC/DC
concepts.definition: Alternating Current (AC) and Direct Current (DC) are the two fundamental types of electrical current, differing in direction of electron flow and voltage characteristics

concepts.label: Torque
concepts.definition: A measure of the rotational force applied to an object, calculated as the product of force and the perpendicular distance from the axis of rotation

concepts.label: Gear
concepts.definition: A rotating machine part having cut teeth that mesh with another toothed part to transmit torque and change speed or direction of rotation

concepts.label: Bearing
concepts.definition: A machine element that constrains relative motion to only the desired motion and reduces friction between moving parts

concepts.label: Spring
concepts.definition: A resilient device that can be pressed or pulled but returns to its former shape when released, storing mechanical energy

concepts.label: Damper
concepts.definition: A device that absorbs kinetic energy and dissipates it, typically used to reduce mechanical oscillations and vibrations

concepts.label: Stress
concepts.definition: The internal force per unit area within materials that arises from externally applied forces, measured in pascals (Pa)

concepts.label: Strain
concepts.definition: The deformation of a material in response to applied stress, expressed as the ratio of change in dimension to the original dimension

concepts.label: Fatigue
concepts.definition: The weakening of a material caused by repeatedly applied loads, leading to progressive crack growth and eventual failure

concepts.label: Thermal Expansion
concepts.definition: The tendency of matter to change its shape, area, volume, and density in response to a change in temperature

concepts.label: Tensile Strength
concepts.definition: The maximum stress that a material can withstand while being stretched or pulled before breaking

concepts.label: Hardness
concepts.definition: A measure of the resistance of a material to localized plastic deformation induced by either mechanical indentation or abrasion

concepts.label: Ductility
concepts.definition: The ability of a material to undergo significant plastic deformation before rupture, often measured by elongation or area reduction

concepts.label: Brittleness
concepts.definition: The tendency of a material to fracture with little or no plastic deformation when subjected to stress

concepts.label: Young's Modulus
concepts.definition: A measure of the stiffness of a solid material, defined as the ratio of stress to strain in the linear elastic region

concepts.label: Poisson Ratio
concepts.definition: The ratio of transverse strain to axial strain when a material is loaded axially, indicating how much a material contracts laterally when stretched

concepts.label: Creep

concepts.label: Slovakia
concepts.definition: Republic in Central Europe, capital Bratislava, population approximately 5.5 million, landlocked country known for castles and mountains

concepts.label: Bratislava
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Slovakia, population approximately 440,000, located on the Danube River near Austrian and Hungarian borders

concepts.label: Slovenia
concepts.definition: Republic in Central Europe, capital Ljubljana, population approximately 2.1 million, small country between Alps and Adriatic Sea

concepts.label: Ljubljana
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Slovenia, population approximately 295,000, green city known for its medieval castle and Baroque architecture

concepts.label: Estonia
concepts.definition: Republic in Northern Europe, capital Tallinn, population approximately 1.3 million, northernmost Baltic state with advanced digital society

concepts.label: Tallinn
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Estonia, population approximately 450,000, medieval city with well-preserved Old Town

concepts.label: Latvia
concepts.definition: Republic in the Baltic region of Northern Europe, capital Riga, population approximately 1.9 million, known for Art Nouveau architecture

concepts.label: Riga
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Latvia, population approximately 630,000, major port city on the Baltic Sea with historic center

concepts.label: Lithuania
concepts.definition: Republic in the Baltic region of Northern Europe, capital Vilnius, population approximately 2.8 million, largest Baltic state

concepts.label: Vilnius
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Lithuania, population approximately 590,000, baroque Old Town with numerous churches and universities

concepts.label: Iran
concepts.definition: Islamic republic in Western Asia, capital Tehran, population approximately 85 million, ancient Persian civilization with rich cultural heritage

concepts.label: Tehran
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Iran, population approximately 15 million in metropolitan area, political and economic center at foot of Alborz Mountains

concepts.label: Iraq
concepts.definition: Federal republic in Western Asia, capital Baghdad, population approximately 41 million, cradle of civilization in Mesopotamia

concepts.label: Baghdad
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Iraq, population approximately 7.2 million, historic city on the Tigris River, center of Islamic Golden Age

concepts.label: Afghanistan
concepts.definition: Islamic emirate in Central and South Asia, capital Kabul, population approximately 40 million, landlocked mountainous country

concepts.label: Kabul
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Afghanistan, population approximately 4.6 million, located in the Kabul Valley in the Hindu Kush mountains

concepts.label: Pakistan
concepts.definition: Islamic republic in South Asia, capital Islamabad, population approximately 230 million, created in 1947 partition of British India

concepts.label: Islamabad
concepts.definition: Capital city of Pakistan, population approximately 1.2 million, planned city built in the 1960s to replace Karachi as capital

concepts.label: Bangladesh
concepts.definition: Parliamentary republic in South Asia, capital Dhaka, population approximately 166 million, formed from East Pakistan in 1971

concepts.label: Dhaka
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Bangladesh, population approximately 22 million in metropolitan area, major river port on the Ganges Delta

concepts.label: Sri Lanka
concepts.definition: Island republic in South Asia, capital Sri Jayawardenepura Kotte, population approximately 22 million, teardrop-shaped island south of India

concepts.label: Sri Jayawardenepura Kotte
concepts.definition: Administrative capital of Sri Lanka, population approximately 120,000, serves as the seat of parliament

concepts.label: Myanmar
concepts.definition: Republic in Southeast Asia, capital Naypyidaw, population approximately 55 million, also known as Burma, rich in natural resources

concepts.label: Naypyidaw
concepts.definition: Capital city of Myanmar, population approximately 1.2 million, planned city built in 2000s to replace Yangon as capital

concepts.label: Cambodia
concepts.definition: Kingdom in Southeast Asia, capital Phnom Penh, population approximately 17 million, home to ancient Khmer Empire and Angkor Wat

concepts.label: Phnom Penh
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Cambodia, population approximately 2.3 million, located at confluence of Mekong and Tonlé Sap rivers

concepts.label: Laos
concepts.definition: Socialist republic in Southeast Asia, capital Vientiane, population approximately 7.3 million, landlocked country known as the Land of a Million Elephants

concepts.label: Vientiane
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Laos, population approximately 950,000, located on the banks of the Mekong River

concepts.label: Brunei
concepts.definition: Absolute monarchy on the island of Borneo, capital Bandar Seri Begawan, population approximately 440,000, wealthy oil-rich sultanate

concepts.label: Bandar Seri Begawan
concepts.definition: Capital city of Brunei, population approximately 180,000, home to the Sultan's palace and numerous mosques

concepts.label: Mongolia
concepts.definition: Landlocked republic in East Asia, capital Ulaanbaatar, population approximately 3.4 million, vast steppes between Russia and China

concepts.label: Ulaanbaatar
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Mongolia, population approximately 1.6 million, coldest capital city in the world

concepts.label: North Korea
concepts.definition: Socialist republic in East Asia, capital Pyongyang, population approximately 26 million, isolated authoritarian state on Korean Peninsula

concepts.label: Pyongyang
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of North Korea, population approximately 3.3 million, planned city with monumental architecture

concepts.label: Kazakhstan
concepts.definition: Republic in Central Asia, capital Nur-Sultan, population approximately 19.4 million, world's largest landlocked country

concepts.label: Nur-Sultan
concepts.definition: Capital city of Kazakhstan, population approximately 1.2 million, futuristic planned city formerly known as Astana

concepts.label: Uzbekistan
concepts.definition: Republic in Central Asia, capital Tashkent, population approximately 35 million, doubly landlocked country on the Silk Road

concepts.label: Tashkent
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Uzbekistan, population approximately 2.6 million, major cultural and economic center of Central Asia

concepts.label: Kyrgyzstan
concepts.definition: Republic in Central Asia, capital Bishkek, population approximately 6.7 million, mountainous landlocked country

concepts.label: Bishkek
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Kyrgyzstan, population approximately 1 million, located in the Chuy Valley near the Kyrgyz Ala-Too range

concepts.label: Tajikistan
concepts.definition: Republic in Central Asia, capital Dushanbe, population approximately 9.8 million, mountainous landlocked country

concepts.label: Dushanbe
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Tajikistan, population approximately 900,000, located in the Hissar Valley

concepts.label: Turkmenistan
concepts.definition: Republic in Central Asia, capital Ashgabat, population approximately 6 million, largely covered by the Karakum Desert

concepts.label: Ashgabat
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Turkmenistan, population approximately 1 million, known for its white marble buildings and golden statues

concepts.label: Jordan
concepts.definition: Constitutional monarchy in Western Asia, capital Amman, population approximately 10.3 million, largely arid country with access to Red Sea

concepts.label: Amman
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Jordan, population approximately 4.3 million in metropolitan area, ancient city built on seven hills

concepts.label: Lebanon
concepts.definition: Republic in Western Asia, capital Beirut, population approximately 6.8 million, small country on the Mediterranean coast

concepts.label: Beirut
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Lebanon, population approximately 2.4 million in metropolitan area, major port and cultural center

concepts.label: Syria
concepts.definition: Republic in Western Asia, capital Damascus, population approximately 18.3 million, ancient land with rich historical heritage

concepts.label: Damascus
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Syria, population approximately 2.3 million, one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world

concepts.label: Israel
concepts.definition: Parliamentary republic in Western Asia, capital Jerusalem, population approximately 9.5 million, Jewish state established in 1948

concepts.label: Jerusalem
concepts.definition: Capital city of Israel, population approximately 950,000, holy city for Judaism, Christianity, and Islam

concepts.label: Palestine
concepts.definition: Partially recognized state in Western Asia, capital East Jerusalem, population approximately 5.2 million, occupied territories

concepts.label: Saudi Arabia
concepts.definition: Absolute monarchy in Western Asia, capital Riyadh, population approximately 35 million, largest country in the Middle East

concepts.label: Riyadh
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Saudi Arabia, population approximately 7.6 million in metropolitan area, political and administrative center

concepts.label: Kuwait
concepts.definition: Constitutional monarchy in Western Asia, capital Kuwait City, population approximately 4.3 million, small oil-rich country on the Persian Gulf

concepts.label: Kuwait City
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Kuwait, population approximately 4.1 million in metropolitan area, major port and financial center

concepts.label: United Arab Emirates
concepts.definition: Federal monarchy in Western Asia, capital Abu Dhabi, population approximately 10 million, federation of seven emirates

concepts.label: Abu Dhabi
concepts.definition: Capital city of the United Arab Emirates, population approximately 1.5 million, largest emirate and oil capital

concepts.label: Qatar
concepts.definition: Absolute monarchy in Western Asia, capital Doha, population approximately 2.9 million, wealthy peninsular country on the Persian Gulf

concepts.label: Doha
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Qatar, population approximately 2.4 million in metropolitan area, modern city with futuristic skyline

concepts.label: Bahrain
concepts.definition: Constitutional monarchy in Western Asia, capital Manama, population approximately 1.7 million, island nation in the Persian Gulf

concepts.label: Manama
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Bahrain, population approximately 650,000, major financial center in the Gulf region

concepts.label: Oman
concepts.definition: Absolute monarchy in Western Asia, capital Muscat, population approximately 5.1 million, sultanate on the Arabian Peninsula

concepts.label: Muscat
concepts.definition: Capital and largest city of Oman, population approximately 1.6 million in metropolitan area, port city on the Gulf of Oman

concepts.label: Yemen
concepts.definition: Republic in Western Asia, capital Sanaa, population approximately 30.5 million, located on the southern tip of the Arabian Peninsula

concepts.label: Sanaa
concepts.definition: Capital city of Yemen, population approximately 2.9 million, one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities, UNESCO World Heritage site

concepts.label: Maldives
