concepts.definition: The property of a conductor to oppose changes in electric current through electromagnetic induction

concepts.label: Quantum Tunneling
concepts.definition: A quantum mechanical phenomenon where particles pass through potential barriers they classically cannot surmount

concepts.label: Spin
concepts.definition: An intrinsic form of angular momentum carried by elementary particles, fundamental to quantum mechanics

concepts.label: Lattice
concepts.definition: The regular, repeating arrangement of atoms, ions, or molecules in a crystalline solid

concepts.label: Phase Transition
concepts.definition: The transformation between different states of matter, such as solid, liquid, gas, or plasma

concepts.label: Electric Motor
concepts.definition: A machine that converts electrical energy into mechanical motion through electromagnetic induction

concepts.label: AC Motor
concepts.definition: An electric motor driven by alternating current, commonly used in industrial applications

concepts.label: DC Motor
concepts.definition: An electric motor that runs on direct current, offering precise speed control

concepts.label: Stepper Motor
concepts.definition: A brushless DC motor that divides full rotation into discrete steps for precise positioning

concepts.label: Induction Motor
concepts.definition: An AC motor where electric current in the rotor is induced by rotating magnetic field

concepts.label: Synchronous Motor
concepts.definition: An AC motor where rotor rotation is synchronized with frequency of supply current

concepts.label: Linear Motor
concepts.definition: An electric motor that produces motion in a straight line rather than rotational motion

concepts.label: Brushless DC Motor
concepts.definition: A synchronous motor using electronic commutation instead of brushes for higher efficiency

concepts.label: Universal Motor
concepts.definition: A commutated series-wound motor that can operate on AC or DC supply

concepts.label: BJT
concepts.definition: Bipolar Junction Transistor - a three-terminal device using both electrons and holes

concepts.label: MOSFET
concepts.definition: Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor Field-Effect Transistor for voltage-controlled switching

concepts.label: JFET
concepts.definition: Junction Field-Effect Transistor with high input impedance and voltage control

concepts.label: IGBT
concepts.definition: Insulated Gate Bipolar Transistor combining MOSFET and BJT characteristics

concepts.label: Operational Amplifier
concepts.definition: High-gain voltage amplifier with differential input and single-ended output

concepts.label: Comparator
concepts.definition: A circuit that compares two voltages and outputs a digital signal

concepts.label: Voltage Regulator
concepts.definition: A circuit designed to maintain a constant voltage level

concepts.label: LED
concepts.definition: Light Emitting Diode - a semiconductor that emits light when current passes through

concepts.label: Resistor
concepts.definition: A passive component that opposes the flow of electric current

concepts.label: Relay
concepts.definition: An electrically operated switch using electromagnetic coil to control contacts

concepts.label: Fuse
concepts.definition: A safety device that melts to break circuit when current exceeds safe levels

concepts.label: Circuit Breaker
concepts.definition: An automatic electrical switch designed to protect circuits from damage

concepts.label: Printed Circuit Board
concepts.definition: A board that mechanically supports and electrically connects electronic components

concepts.label: IC
concepts.definition: Integrated Circuit - a semiconductor containing multiple interconnected electronic components

concepts.label: Microcontroller
concepts.definition: A small computer on a single integrated circuit containing processor, memory and peripherals

concepts.label: RAM
concepts.definition: Random Access Memory - volatile memory for temporary data storage during processing

concepts.label: ROM
concepts.definition: Read-Only Memory - non-volatile memory typically containing firmware or boot instructions

concepts.label: SSD
concepts.definition: Solid State Drive - data storage device using integrated circuit assemblies

concepts.label: HDD
concepts.definition: Hard Disk Drive - data storage using magnetic storage on rotating disks

concepts.label: Cache Memory
concepts.definition: High-speed memory buffer between CPU and main memory for faster access

concepts.label: Motherboard
concepts.definition: Main circuit board connecting all computer components together

concepts.label: Power Supply Unit
concepts.definition: Device that converts AC mains power to regulated DC power for computer components

concepts.label: Heat Sink
concepts.definition: Passive heat exchanger that transfers heat from electronic components to air

concepts.label: Network Interface Card
concepts.definition: Hardware component that connects computer to network

concepts.label: Ethernet
concepts.definition: Family of wired networking technologies for local area networks

concepts.label: WiFi
concepts.definition: Wireless networking technology using radio waves for local area connectivity

concepts.label: Bluetooth
concepts.definition: Short-range wireless technology for connecting devices over short distances

concepts.label: Modem
concepts.definition: Device that modulates and demodulates signals for data transmission

concepts.label: Internal Combustion Engine
concepts.definition: Heat engine where combustion of fuel occurs within engine cylinder

concepts.label: Four Stroke Engine
concepts.definition: Internal combustion engine with intake, compression, power and exhaust strokes

concepts.label: Two Stroke Engine
concepts.definition: Internal combustion engine completing power cycle in two piston strokes

concepts.label: Diesel Engine
concepts.definition: Compression ignition engine using diesel fuel for combustion

concepts.label: Gasoline Engine
concepts.definition: Spark ignition engine using gasoline as fuel

concepts.label: Turbocharger
concepts.definition: Forced induction device that increases engine power using exhaust gases

concepts.label: Supercharger
concepts.definition: Mechanically driven forced induction device for increasing engine power

concepts.label: Fuel Injection
concepts.definition: System that delivers fuel into combustion chamber or intake manifold

concepts.label: Catalytic Converter
concepts.definition: Emission control device that reduces toxic gases in exhaust

concepts.label: Manual Transmission
concepts.definition: Multi-speed transmission where gear changes are made manually by driver

concepts.label: Automatic Transmission
concepts.definition: Multi-speed transmission that changes gears automatically

concepts.label: CVT
concepts.definition: Continuously Variable Transmission providing seamless range of gear ratios

concepts.label: Differential
concepts.definition: Gear system allowing wheels to rotate at different speeds while cornering

concepts.label: Torque Converter
concepts.definition: Fluid coupling device for transferring rotating power from engine to transmission

concepts.label: Planetary Gear
concepts.definition: Gear system with central sun gear surrounded by planet gears

concepts.label: Worm Gear
concepts.definition: Gear arrangement with worm screw meshing with worm wheel

concepts.label: Helical Gear
concepts.definition: Cylindrical gear with teeth cut at angle to axis for smooth operation

concepts.label: Bevel Gear
concepts.definition: Conical gear for transmitting power between intersecting shafts

concepts.label: Hydraulic System
concepts.definition: Power transmission system using pressurized liquid to generate mechanical force

concepts.label: Hydraulic Pump
concepts.definition: Mechanical device that converts mechanical power into hydraulic energy

concepts.label: Hydraulic Cylinder
concepts.definition: Linear actuator powered by pressurized hydraulic fluid

concepts.label: Hydraulic Valve
concepts.definition: Device for controlling direction, pressure, and flow rate of hydraulic fluid

concepts.label: Pneumatic System
concepts.definition: Power transmission system using compressed air to generate mechanical force

concepts.label: Air Compressor
concepts.definition: Machine that converts power into potential energy stored in pressurized air

concepts.label: Pneumatic Cylinder
concepts.definition: Linear actuator powered by compressed air

concepts.label: Pressure Relief Valve
concepts.definition: Safety device that relieves pressure when it exceeds set limit

concepts.label: Accumulator
concepts.definition: Energy storage device that stores fluid under pressure

concepts.label: Ball Bearing
concepts.definition: Rolling element bearing using balls to maintain separation between races

concepts.label: Roller Bearing
concepts.definition: Rolling element bearing using cylindrical rollers instead of balls

concepts.label: Thrust Bearing
concepts.definition: Bearing designed to handle axial loads rather than radial loads

concepts.label: Journal Bearing
concepts.definition: Plain bearing that supports rotating shaft in radial direction

concepts.label: O-Ring
concepts.definition: Circular sealing element with round cross-section for static and dynamic sealing

concepts.label: Gasket
concepts.definition: Mechanical seal filling space between two objects to prevent leakage

concepts.label: Coil Spring
concepts.definition: Helical spring wound in coil form for compression or tension applications

concepts.label: Solar Panel
concepts.definition: Device that converts sunlight into electricity using photovoltaic effect

concepts.label: Solar Cell
concepts.definition: Electronic device that converts light energy directly into electrical energy

concepts.label: Wind Turbine
concepts.definition: Device that converts kinetic energy from wind into electrical energy

concepts.label: Wind Generator
concepts.definition: Electrical generator driven by wind turbine for producing electricity

concepts.label: Hydroelectric Generator
concepts.definition: Generator powered by flowing water to produce electricity

concepts.label: Nuclear Reactor
concepts.definition: Device to initiate and control sustained nuclear chain reaction for energy

concepts.label: Steam Turbine
concepts.definition: Turbine driven by steam pressure to generate rotational mechanical energy

concepts.label: Gas Turbine
concepts.definition: Turbine engine using combustion gases to drive compressor and generate power

concepts.label: Lithium Ion Battery
concepts.definition: Rechargeable battery where lithium ions move between anode and cathode

concepts.label: Lead Acid Battery
concepts.definition: Rechargeable battery using lead and sulfuric acid for automotive applications

concepts.label: Fuel Cell
concepts.definition: Device that generates electricity through chemical reaction of hydrogen and oxygen

concepts.label: Capacitor Bank
concepts.definition: Group of capacitors connected to store large amounts of electrical energy

concepts.label: Flywheel
concepts.definition: Rotating mechanical device for storing kinetic energy

concepts.label: Inverter
concepts.definition: Electronic device that converts DC power to AC power

concepts.label: Rectifier
concepts.definition: Electronic device that converts AC power to DC power

concepts.label: UPS
concepts.definition: Uninterruptible Power Supply providing backup power during outages

concepts.label: Power Grid
concepts.definition: Interconnected network for delivering electricity from producers to consumers

concepts.label: Reinforced Concrete
concepts.definition: Concrete strengthened with steel rebar to handle tension and compression

concepts.label: Prestressed Concrete
concepts.definition: Concrete compressed by tensioned cables before loading to improve performance

concepts.label: Rebar
concepts.definition: Steel reinforcement bar used to strengthen concrete in tension

concepts.label: Beam
concepts.definition: Structural element that withstands loads primarily by resisting bending

concepts.label: Column
concepts.definition: Vertical structural element designed to transmit compressive loads

concepts.label: Truss
concepts.definition: Framework of beams forming triangular units for structural strength

concepts.label: Arch
concepts.definition: Curved structural element spanning opening and supporting load above

concepts.label: Welding
concepts.definition: Process of joining materials by melting and fusing them together

concepts.label: Arc Welding
concepts.definition: Welding process using electric arc to melt and join metals

concepts.label: TIG Welding
concepts.definition: Tungsten Inert Gas welding using tungsten electrode and inert gas shielding

concepts.label: MIG Welding
concepts.definition: Metal Inert Gas welding using consumable wire electrode and gas shielding

concepts.label: Soldering
concepts.definition: Process of joining metals using molten filler metal at low temperatures

concepts.label: Brazing
concepts.definition: Metal joining process using molten filler metal at temperatures above 450°C

concepts.label: Machining
concepts.definition: Manufacturing process removing material to create desired shape and finish

concepts.label: CNC Machine
concepts.definition: Computer Numerical Control machine for automated machining operations

concepts.label: Lathe
concepts.definition: Machine tool for rotating workpiece while cutting tool shapes the material

concepts.label: Milling Machine
concepts.definition: Machine tool using rotating cutters to remove material from workpiece

concepts.label: 3D Printer
concepts.definition: Additive manufacturing device creating objects by depositing material layer by layer

concepts.label: FDM Printing
concepts.definition: Fused Deposition Modeling - 3D printing by extruding thermoplastic filament

concepts.label: SLA Printing
concepts.definition: Stereolithography - 3D printing using laser to cure photopolymer resin

concepts.label: SLS Printing
concepts.definition: Selective Laser Sintering - 3D printing by sintering powder with laser

concepts.label: CAD
concepts.definition: Computer Aided Design - software for creating technical drawings and models

concepts.label: CAM
concepts.definition: Computer Aided Manufacturing - software for generating machine tool paths

concepts.label: CAE

concepts.label: Newton's First Law

concepts.label: Newton's Second Law
concepts.definition: Force equals mass times acceleration (F=ma)

concepts.label: Newton's Third Law
concepts.definition: Every action has an equal and opposite reaction

concepts.label: Impulse

concepts.label: Kinetic Energy
concepts.definition: Energy of motion equal to half mass times velocity squared

concepts.label: Potential Energy
concepts.definition: Energy stored due to position or configuration

concepts.label: Gravitational Potential Energy
concepts.definition: Energy stored due to height in a gravitational field

concepts.label: Angular Momentum
concepts.definition: Rotational equivalent of linear momentum

concepts.label: Angular Velocity
concepts.definition: Rate of change of angular displacement

concepts.label: Centripetal Force
concepts.definition: Force directed toward center of circular motion

concepts.label: Centrifugal Force
concepts.definition: Apparent outward force in a rotating reference frame

concepts.label: Static Friction
concepts.definition: Friction preventing onset of relative motion between surfaces

concepts.label: Kinetic Friction
concepts.definition: Friction acting on objects in relative motion

concepts.label: Elastic Collision
concepts.definition: Collision where kinetic energy is conserved

concepts.label: Inelastic Collision
concepts.definition: Collision where kinetic energy is not conserved

concepts.label: Conservation of Energy
concepts.definition: Total energy in an isolated system remains constant

concepts.label: Conservation of Momentum
concepts.definition: Total momentum in an isolated system remains constant

concepts.label: Simple Harmonic Motion
concepts.definition: Oscillatory motion where restoring force is proportional to displacement

concepts.label: Hooke's Law
concepts.definition: Force exerted by spring proportional to displacement from equilibrium

concepts.label: Pendulum

concepts.definition: Fundamental constant G in Newton's law of gravitation

concepts.definition: Motion under influence of gravity alone

concepts.label: Terminal Velocity

concepts.label: Drag Force

concepts.definition: Upward force on object submerged in fluid

concepts.definition: Buoyant force equals weight of displaced fluid

concepts.label: Pascal's Principle
concepts.definition: Pressure applied to confined fluid transmits equally throughout

concepts.label: Bernoulli's Principle
concepts.definition: Increase in fluid speed causes decrease in pressure

concepts.definition: Movement of liquid through narrow spaces against gravity

concepts.label: Plasticity

concepts.label: First Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: Energy cannot be created or destroyed only transformed

concepts.label: Second Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: Entropy of an isolated system always increases over time

concepts.label: Third Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: Entropy approaches zero as temperature approaches absolute zero

concepts.label: Zeroth Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: If two systems are in thermal equilibrium with a third they are in equilibrium with each other

concepts.label: Carnot Efficiency

concepts.label: Specific Heat Capacity

concepts.label: Latent Heat
concepts.definition: Energy absorbed or released during a phase change

concepts.label: Ideal Gas Law
concepts.definition: PV=nRT relating pressure volume temperature and amount of ideal gas

concepts.label: Boyle's Law
concepts.definition: At constant temperature pressure and volume are inversely proportional

concepts.label: Charles's Law
concepts.definition: At constant pressure volume is proportional to temperature

concepts.label: Avogadro's Law
concepts.definition: Equal volumes of gases at same conditions contain equal numbers of molecules

concepts.label: Stefan-Boltzmann Law
concepts.definition: Total energy radiated is proportional to fourth power of temperature

concepts.label: Black Body Radiation
concepts.definition: Electromagnetic radiation emitted by idealized opaque body

concepts.label: Sublimation

concepts.label: Melting Point

concepts.label: Triple Point

concepts.label: Critical Point

concepts.label: Adiabatic Process

concepts.label: Isothermal Process
concepts.definition: Thermodynamic process at constant temperature

concepts.label: Isobaric Process
concepts.definition: Thermodynamic process at constant pressure

concepts.label: Isochoric Process
concepts.definition: Thermodynamic process at constant volume

concepts.label: Electric Charge

concepts.label: Coulomb's Law

concepts.label: Electric Field

concepts.label: Electric Potential

concepts.label: Electric Current

concepts.label: Magnetic Field
concepts.definition: Region of space where moving charges experience a force

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Induction

concepts.label: Faraday's Law

concepts.label: Lenz's Law

concepts.label: Ampere's Law

concepts.label: Speed of Light

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Spectrum

concepts.label: Microwaves

concepts.label: Infrared Radiation

concepts.label: X-rays

concepts.label: Gamma Rays

concepts.label: Reflection

concepts.label: Schrodinger Equation

concepts.label: Quantum Superposition

concepts.label: Photoelectric Effect

concepts.definition: Fundamental constant relating photon energy to frequency

concepts.label: Fermion

concepts.label: Higgs Field

concepts.label: Weak Nuclear Force

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Force

concepts.label: Hedge Fund

concepts.label: Venture Capital

concepts.label: Private Equity

concepts.label: Option (Finance)

concepts.label: Futures Contract
concepts.definition: Agreement to buy or sell an asset at a predetermined future date and price

concepts.label: Short Selling
concepts.definition: Selling borrowed securities with intent to repurchase at a lower price

concepts.label: Arbitrage
concepts.definition: Practice of profiting from price differences in different markets

concepts.label: Risk Management
concepts.definition: Process of identifying, assessing, and controlling threats to capital

concepts.label: Return on Investment
concepts.definition: Ratio measuring gain or loss relative to investment cost

concepts.label: Simple Interest
concepts.definition: Interest calculated only on the principal amount

concepts.label: Mortgage
concepts.definition: Loan secured by real property used to purchase real estate

concepts.label: Credit Score
concepts.definition: Numerical expression of creditworthiness based on credit history analysis

concepts.label: Keynesian Economics
concepts.definition: Theory that aggregate demand is the primary driver of economic activity

concepts.label: Monetarism
concepts.definition: Theory emphasizing the role of money supply in controlling economic activity

concepts.label: Austrian Economics
concepts.definition: School of economics emphasizing individual action and spontaneous order

concepts.label: Supply-Side Economics
concepts.definition: Theory that lower taxes and deregulation stimulate economic growth

concepts.label: Adam Smith
concepts.definition: Scottish economist considered the father of modern economics

concepts.label: Invisible Hand

concepts.label: Free Market
concepts.definition: Economic system with minimal government intervention in transactions

concepts.label: Perfect Competition

concepts.label: Public Good
concepts.definition: Good that is non-excludable and non-rivalrous in consumption

concepts.label: Tragedy of the Commons

concepts.label: Prisoner's Dilemma

concepts.label: Prospect Theory
concepts.definition: Theory describing how people choose between probabilistic alternatives involving risk

concepts.label: Loss Aversion

concepts.label: Moral Hazard
concepts.definition: Risk that a party insulated from risk behaves differently than if fully exposed

concepts.label: Adverse Selection
concepts.definition: Market situation where asymmetric information leads to bad outcomes

concepts.label: Trade Deficit

concepts.label: Trade Surplus

concepts.label: Tariff

concepts.label: Free Trade
concepts.definition: Trade policy allowing unrestricted import and export of goods between countries

concepts.label: Exchange Rate
concepts.definition: Rate at which one currency can be exchanged for another

concepts.label: Forex Market
concepts.definition: Global decentralized market for trading national currencies

concepts.label: Balance of Payments
concepts.definition: Record of all financial transactions between a country and the rest of the world

concepts.label: World Bank
concepts.definition: International financial institution providing loans and grants to developing countries

concepts.label: IMF
concepts.definition: International Monetary Fund promoting global monetary cooperation

concepts.label: WTO
concepts.definition: World Trade Organization governing international trade rules

concepts.label: Progressive Tax
concepts.definition: Tax system where rate increases as taxable amount increases

concepts.label: Regressive Tax
concepts.definition: Tax system where rate decreases as taxable amount increases

concepts.label: Capital Gains Tax
concepts.definition: Tax on profit from the sale of an asset

concepts.label: National Debt
concepts.definition: Total amount of money owed by a country's government

concepts.label: Budget Deficit
concepts.definition: Excess of expenditure over revenue in a given period

concepts.label: Microeconomics
concepts.definition: Study of individual and business decisions regarding resource allocation

concepts.label: Macroeconomics
concepts.definition: Study of economy-wide phenomena including inflation, unemployment, and GDP

concepts.label: Econometrics
concepts.definition: Application of statistical methods to economic data for testing theories

concepts.label: Gini Coefficient

concepts.label: Purchasing Power Parity
concepts.definition: Theory that exchange rates should equalize prices of identical goods

concepts.label: Phonetics
concepts.definition: Study of the physical sounds of human speech

concepts.label: Morphology (Linguistics)
concepts.definition: Study of the structure and formation of words

concepts.label: Adverb
concepts.definition: Word class that modifies a verb, adjective, or other adverb

concepts.label: Pronoun
concepts.definition: Word that substitutes for a noun or noun phrase

concepts.label: Preposition
concepts.definition: Word governing a noun or pronoun expressing a relation to another word

concepts.label: Conjunction
concepts.definition: Word connecting clauses, sentences, or words

concepts.label: Subject (Grammar)

concepts.label: Tense

concepts.label: Voice (Grammar)

concepts.label: Mood (Grammar)

concepts.label: Conjugation

concepts.label: Lexicography

concepts.label: Sociolinguistics

concepts.label: Historical Linguistics

concepts.label: Computational Linguistics

concepts.label: Creole Language

concepts.label: Indo-European Languages

concepts.label: Sino-Tibetan Languages

concepts.label: Afroasiatic Languages

concepts.label: Niger-Congo Languages

concepts.label: Austronesian Languages

concepts.label: Proto-Indo-European

concepts.label: Alphabet

concepts.label: Latin Alphabet
concepts.definition: Writing system used for most Western European languages

concepts.label: Cyrillic Alphabet

concepts.label: Arabic Script

concepts.label: Chinese Characters

concepts.label: Logogram

concepts.label: Syllabary

concepts.label: Vowel

concepts.label: Consonant

concepts.label: Tone (Linguistics)

concepts.label: Bilingualism

concepts.label: Language Acquisition

concepts.label: Universal Grammar

concepts.label: Sculpture

concepts.label: Architecture (Art)

concepts.label: Ceramics

concepts.label: Drama

concepts.label: Essay

concepts.definition: Literary genre blending magical elements with realistic narrative

concepts.label: Dystopia

concepts.label: Guernica

concepts.label: Hamlet

concepts.label: Don Quixote

concepts.label: Mercury (Planet)

concepts.label: Gas Giant

concepts.label: Ice Giant

concepts.label: Orbital Period

concepts.label: Luminosity

concepts.label: Apparent Magnitude

concepts.label: Elliptical Galaxy

concepts.label: LIGO

concepts.label: Habitable Zone

concepts.label: Cosmology

concepts.label: Astrophysics

concepts.label: Radio Telescope

concepts.label: Space Shuttle

concepts.label: Voyager Program

concepts.label: Orbit

concepts.label: Escape Velocity
concepts.definition: Minimum speed needed for an object to escape gravitational pull

concepts.label: Space Debris

concepts.label: Rocket Propulsion

concepts.label: Metaphysics

concepts.label: Logic (Philosophy)

concepts.label: Aesthetics

concepts.label: Political Philosophy
concepts.definition: Study of fundamental questions about government, politics, and justice

concepts.label: Philosophy of Mind
concepts.definition: Branch examining the nature of the mind, consciousness, and mental events

concepts.label: Philosophy of Science
concepts.definition: Branch examining the foundations, methods, and implications of science

concepts.label: Philosophy of Language
concepts.definition: Branch examining the nature, origin, and use of language

concepts.label: Socrates

concepts.label: Plato
concepts.definition: Greek philosopher, student of Socrates, founder of the Academy, author of The Republic

concepts.label: Aristotle

concepts.label: René Descartes

concepts.label: Immanuel Kant

concepts.label: Friedrich Nietzsche

concepts.label: John Locke

concepts.label: David Hume

concepts.label: Jean-Paul Sartre

concepts.label: Simone de Beauvoir
concepts.definition: French existentialist philosopher and feminist, author of The Second Sex

concepts.label: Ludwig Wittgenstein
concepts.definition: Austrian-British philosopher known for work on logic and language

concepts.label: Karl Marx
concepts.definition: German philosopher whose ideas formed the basis of communism

concepts.label: Thomas Aquinas
concepts.definition: Italian philosopher who synthesized Aristotelian philosophy with Christianity

concepts.label: Confucius
concepts.definition: Chinese philosopher whose teachings emphasize morality, social harmony, and justice

concepts.label: Laozi
concepts.definition: Chinese philosopher traditionally regarded as founder of Taoism

concepts.label: Siddhartha Gautama
concepts.definition: Founder of Buddhism, known as the Buddha

concepts.label: John Stuart Mill
concepts.definition: British philosopher known for utilitarianism and liberalism

concepts.label: Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel
concepts.definition: German idealist philosopher known for dialectical method

concepts.label: Martin Heidegger
concepts.definition: German philosopher known for existential phenomenology and Being and Time

concepts.label: Socratic Method

concepts.label: Platonic Idealism
concepts.definition: Theory that abstract forms or ideas are more real than material objects

concepts.label: Dualism

concepts.label: Übermensch

concepts.label: Will to Power

concepts.label: Tabula Rasa

concepts.label: Existentialism

concepts.label: Nihilism

concepts.label: Consequentialism

concepts.label: Stoicism

concepts.label: Epicureanism

concepts.label: Skepticism

concepts.label: Pragmatism

concepts.label: Phenomenology

concepts.label: Dialectic

concepts.label: Logical Fallacy

concepts.label: Leavening
concepts.definition: Process of adding gas to dough to make it rise

concepts.label: Reduction (Cooking)
concepts.definition: Thickening a liquid by simmering to evaporate water and concentrate flavor

concepts.label: French Cuisine
concepts.definition: Cooking traditions of France, known for techniques and sauces

concepts.label: Italian Cuisine
concepts.definition: Cooking traditions of Italy, known for pasta, pizza, and regional diversity

concepts.label: Japanese Cuisine
concepts.definition: Cooking traditions of Japan emphasizing seasonal ingredients and presentation

concepts.label: Chinese Cuisine
concepts.definition: Cooking traditions of China with diverse regional styles

concepts.label: Indian Cuisine
concepts.definition: Cooking traditions of India known for spices and diverse regional dishes

concepts.label: Mexican Cuisine
concepts.definition: Cooking traditions of Mexico blending indigenous and Spanish influences

concepts.label: Thai Cuisine
concepts.definition: Cooking traditions of Thailand balancing sweet, sour, salty, bitter, and spicy

concepts.label: Mediterranean Diet
concepts.definition: Dietary pattern based on traditional foods of Mediterranean countries

concepts.label: Yeast
concepts.definition: Unicellular fungus used in baking and brewing as a leavening and fermenting agent

concepts.label: Salt (Cooking)
concepts.definition: Mineral used universally for seasoning and preserving food

concepts.label: Soy Sauce
concepts.definition: Liquid condiment of Chinese origin made from fermented soybeans

concepts.label: Macronutrient
concepts.definition: Nutrient required in large amounts: carbohydrates, proteins, and fats

concepts.label: Micronutrient
concepts.definition: Nutrient required in small amounts: vitamins and minerals

concepts.label: Protein (Nutrition)
concepts.definition: Macronutrient composed of amino acids essential for growth and repair

concepts.label: Fat (Nutrition)
concepts.definition: Macronutrient providing concentrated energy and essential fatty acids

concepts.label: Saturated Fat
concepts.definition: Fat with no double bonds between carbon atoms, typically solid at room temperature

concepts.label: Unsaturated Fat
concepts.definition: Fat with one or more double bonds, typically liquid at room temperature

concepts.label: Trans Fat
concepts.definition: Unsaturated fat with trans configuration, associated with increased heart disease risk

concepts.label: Omega-3 Fatty Acids
concepts.definition: Essential polyunsaturated fats important for brain and heart health

concepts.label: Essential Amino Acid
concepts.definition: Amino acid that cannot be synthesized by the body and must come from food

concepts.label: Vitamin A
concepts.definition: Fat-soluble vitamin essential for vision, immune function, and skin health

concepts.label: Vitamin E
concepts.definition: Fat-soluble antioxidant vitamin protecting cells from oxidative damage

concepts.label: Vitamin K
concepts.definition: Fat-soluble vitamin essential for blood clotting and bone metabolism

concepts.label: Iron (Nutrient)
concepts.definition: Essential mineral for oxygen transport in blood via hemoglobin

concepts.label: Fiber (Nutrition)
concepts.definition: Indigestible carbohydrate promoting digestive health and satiety

concepts.label: BMI
concepts.definition: Body Mass Index, measure of body fat based on height and weight

concepts.label: Gluten
concepts.definition: Proteins in wheat that give dough its elastic texture

concepts.label: Probiotic
concepts.definition: Live microorganisms that provide health benefits when consumed

concepts.label: Prebiotic
concepts.definition: Non-digestible food ingredients that promote growth of beneficial gut bacteria

concepts.label: Vegetarianism
concepts.definition: Diet excluding meat but potentially including dairy and eggs

concepts.label: Veganism
concepts.definition: Diet and lifestyle excluding all animal products

concepts.label: Ketogenic Diet
concepts.definition: High-fat, low-carbohydrate diet that induces ketosis

concepts.label: Paleo Diet
concepts.definition: Diet based on foods presumed eaten by early humans

concepts.label: Intermittent Fasting
concepts.definition: Eating pattern cycling between periods of fasting and eating

concepts.label: DASH Diet
concepts.definition: Dietary approach to stop hypertension through low sodium and balanced nutrition

concepts.label: Food Allergy
concepts.definition: Immune system reaction to certain foods causing adverse symptoms

concepts.label: Lactose Intolerance
concepts.definition: Inability to digest lactose, the sugar in milk and dairy products

concepts.label: Athletics
concepts.definition: Competitive sporting events involving running, jumping, throwing, and walking

concepts.label: Olympic Games
concepts.definition: International multi-sport event held every four years

concepts.label: FIFA World Cup
concepts.definition: International football (soccer) tournament held every four years

concepts.label: Super Bowl
concepts.definition: Annual championship game of the NFL in American football

concepts.label: Marathon
concepts.definition: Long-distance running race of 42.195 kilometers (26.2 miles)

concepts.label: Sprint
concepts.definition: Short-distance running race emphasizing maximum speed

concepts.label: Football (Soccer)
concepts.definition: Sport played between two teams of 11 kicking a ball into a goal

concepts.label: Golf
concepts.definition: Club-and-ball sport where players hit balls into holes in fewest strokes

concepts.label: Boxing
concepts.definition: Combat sport where two people throw punches at each other in a ring

concepts.label: Martial Arts
concepts.definition: Codified systems of combat practices for self-defense, exercise, and sport

concepts.label: Judo
concepts.definition: Japanese martial art and Olympic sport focusing on throwing and grappling

concepts.label: Karate
concepts.definition: Japanese martial art using strikes, kicks, and blocks

concepts.label: Taekwondo
concepts.definition: Korean martial art characterized by head-height kicks and jumping/spinning kicks

concepts.label: Wrestling
concepts.definition: Combat sport involving grappling techniques like throws, pins, and holds

concepts.label: Baseball
concepts.definition: Bat-and-ball sport played between two teams of nine players

concepts.label: American Football
concepts.definition: Team sport played on a rectangular field with goalposts at each end

concepts.label: Rugby
concepts.definition: Team sport where players carry, pass, and kick an oval ball to score

concepts.label: Ice Hockey
concepts.definition: Sport played on ice with sticks and a puck between two teams

concepts.label: Volleyball
concepts.definition: Team sport where two teams hit a ball over a net

concepts.label: Table Tennis
concepts.definition: Sport where players hit a lightweight ball over a net on a hard table

concepts.label: Badminton
concepts.definition: Racket sport played with a shuttlecock over a net

concepts.label: Tour de France
concepts.definition: Annual men's multiple-stage bicycle race primarily held in France

concepts.label: Gymnastics
concepts.definition: Sport involving exercises requiring balance, strength, flexibility, and coordination

concepts.label: Figure Skating
concepts.definition: Sport combining jumps, spins, and artistic movements on ice

concepts.label: Rock Climbing
concepts.definition: Sport of climbing natural or artificial rock walls

concepts.label: Weightlifting
concepts.definition: Sport of lifting heavy weights in prescribed manners

concepts.label: Triathlon
concepts.definition: Multi-sport race combining swimming, cycling, and running

concepts.label: Decathlon
concepts.definition: Combined athletic event of ten track and field events

concepts.label: Formula One
concepts.definition: Highest class of international single-seater auto racing

concepts.label: NASCAR
concepts.definition: National Association for Stock Car Auto Racing

concepts.label: Esports
concepts.definition: Competitive video gaming at an organized, multiplayer level

concepts.label: Chess
concepts.definition: Two-player strategy board game played on a checkered board

concepts.label: Yoga
concepts.definition: Physical, mental, and spiritual practices originating in ancient India

concepts.label: Pilates
concepts.definition: Exercise system focusing on core strength, flexibility, and body awareness

concepts.label: CrossFit
concepts.definition: High-intensity fitness regimen combining elements of various sports and exercises

concepts.label: Aerobic Exercise
concepts.definition: Physical exercise of low to moderate intensity relying primarily on oxygen metabolism

concepts.label: Anaerobic Exercise
concepts.definition: High-intensity exercise exceeding oxygen supply, using stored energy

concepts.label: VO2 Max
concepts.definition: Maximum rate of oxygen consumption during incremental exercise

concepts.label: Lactic Acid
concepts.definition: Byproduct of anaerobic metabolism during intense exercise

concepts.label: Muscle Fiber
concepts.definition: Individual cells within a muscle, classified as Type I (slow) or Type II (fast)

concepts.label: Plyometrics
concepts.definition: Exercise involving rapid stretching and contracting of muscles for explosive power

concepts.label: Biomechanics
concepts.definition: Study of mechanical laws relating to movement of living organisms

concepts.label: Sports Psychology
concepts.definition: Study of how psychological factors affect sports performance

concepts.label: Doping
concepts.definition: Use of banned substances or methods to enhance athletic performance

concepts.label: WADA
concepts.definition: World Anti-Doping Agency, organization fighting doping in sports

concepts.label: Concussion
concepts.definition: Traumatic brain injury caused by a blow to the head affecting brain function

concepts.label: ACL Tear
concepts.definition: Injury to the anterior cruciate ligament in the knee, common in sports

concepts.label: Warm-Up
concepts.definition: Low-intensity exercise before a workout to prepare the body

concepts.label: Cool-Down
concepts.definition: Light exercise after workout to gradually reduce heart rate

concepts.label: Overtraining
concepts.definition: Excessive training leading to decreased performance and increased injury risk

concepts.label: Constitution
concepts.definition: Fundamental principles or established precedents defining governance of a state

concepts.label: Common Law
concepts.definition: Legal system based on judicial precedent rather than statutory laws

concepts.label: Civil Law System
concepts.definition: Legal system based on comprehensive written codes and statutes

concepts.label: Sharia Law
concepts.definition: Religious law forming part of the Islamic tradition derived from the Quran

concepts.label: Statute
concepts.definition: Written law passed by a legislative body

concepts.label: Precedent
concepts.definition: Legal principle established by prior court decisions guiding future cases

concepts.label: Jurisdiction
concepts.definition: Official power to make legal decisions and judgments

concepts.label: Due Process
concepts.definition: Legal requirement that the state must respect all legal rights owed to a person

concepts.label: Rule of Law
concepts.definition: Principle that all persons and institutions are accountable to laws equally applied

concepts.label: Habeas Corpus
concepts.definition: Legal principle requiring a person under arrest to be brought before a judge

concepts.label: Presumption of Innocence
concepts.definition: Principle that a person is innocent until proven guilty

concepts.label: Burden of Proof
concepts.definition: Obligation to prove an assertion or claim in legal proceedings

concepts.label: Criminal Law
concepts.definition: Body of law relating to crime and punishment

concepts.label: Civil Law (Private)
concepts.definition: Law dealing with disputes between individuals or organizations

concepts.label: Constitutional Law
concepts.definition: Body of law dealing with interpretation and implementation of a constitution

concepts.label: International Law
concepts.definition: Set of rules generally accepted as binding between nations

concepts.label: Human Rights
concepts.definition: Rights inherent to all human beings regardless of race, sex, nationality, or religion

concepts.label: Universal Declaration of Human Rights
concepts.definition: 1948 UN document proclaiming inalienable rights of all human beings

concepts.label: Geneva Conventions
concepts.definition: International treaties governing the treatment of civilians and prisoners of war

concepts.label: Treaty
concepts.definition: Formal agreement between states governed by international law

concepts.label: Contract Law
concepts.definition: Body of law governing enforceable agreements between parties

concepts.label: Tort Law
concepts.definition: Body of law addressing civil wrongs that cause harm to others

concepts.label: Property Law
concepts.definition: Area of law governing ownership and use of property

concepts.label: Intellectual Property
concepts.definition: Legal rights to creations of the mind including patents, copyrights, and trademarks

concepts.label: Patent
concepts.definition: Exclusive right granted for an invention for a limited period

concepts.label: Copyright
concepts.definition: Legal right of the creator of an original work to control its use

concepts.label: Trademark
concepts.definition: Recognizable sign, design, or expression identifying products from a particular source

concepts.label: Trade Secret
concepts.definition: Confidential business information providing a competitive edge

concepts.label: Labor Law
concepts.definition: Body of law governing the relationship between employers and employees

concepts.label: Environmental Law
concepts.definition: Body of law addressing the effects of human activity on the environment

concepts.label: Tax Law
concepts.definition: Area of law governing the legal obligations for paying taxes

concepts.label: Corporate Law
concepts.definition: Body of law governing the rights and obligations of corporations

concepts.label: Antitrust Law
concepts.definition: Law promoting competition and preventing monopolistic practices

concepts.label: Bankruptcy
concepts.definition: Legal process for individuals or businesses unable to repay outstanding debts

concepts.label: Republic
concepts.definition: Form of government where head of state is elected rather than hereditary

concepts.label: Monarchy
concepts.definition: Form of government with a monarch as head of state

concepts.label: Constitutional Monarchy
concepts.definition: Monarchy where ruler's powers are limited by a constitution

concepts.label: Dictatorship
concepts.definition: Form of government where one person holds absolute power

concepts.label: Authoritarianism
concepts.definition: Government characterized by strong central power and limited political freedoms

concepts.label: Totalitarianism
concepts.definition: Government system demanding complete subservience to the state

concepts.label: Federalism
concepts.definition: System dividing power between central and regional governments

concepts.label: Separation of Powers
concepts.definition: Division of government into branches with separate powers and responsibilities

concepts.label: Legislature
concepts.definition: Deliberative assembly with authority to make laws

concepts.label: Executive Branch
concepts.definition: Branch of government responsible for implementing and enforcing laws

concepts.label: Judiciary
concepts.definition: System of courts interpreting and applying the law

concepts.label: Parliament
concepts.definition: Legislative body in countries with parliamentary systems of government

concepts.label: Congress
concepts.definition: National legislative body, particularly of the United States

concepts.label: Senate
concepts.definition: Upper chamber in a bicameral legislature

concepts.label: Supreme Court
concepts.definition: Highest court in a judicial system

concepts.label: Cabinet (Government)
concepts.definition: Group of senior officials advising the head of government

concepts.label: Bureaucracy
concepts.definition: System of government with many administrators and complex rules

concepts.label: Political Party
concepts.definition: Organized group seeking to influence government policy through elections

concepts.label: Suffrage
concepts.definition: Right to vote in political elections

concepts.label: Referendum
concepts.definition: Direct vote in which an entire electorate votes on a particular proposal

concepts.label: Lobbying
concepts.definition: Attempting to influence government decisions by organized groups

concepts.label: Diplomacy
concepts.definition: Practice of conducting negotiations between representatives of states

concepts.label: NATO
concepts.definition: North Atlantic Treaty Organization, military alliance of North American and European nations

concepts.label: Sovereignty
concepts.definition: Supreme authority of a state to govern itself

concepts.label: Geopolitics
concepts.definition: Study of the effects of geography on politics and international relations

concepts.label: Socialism
concepts.definition: Political and economic system with social ownership of means of production

concepts.label: Liberalism
concepts.definition: Political philosophy emphasizing individual rights, liberty, and equality

concepts.label: Conservatism
concepts.definition: Political philosophy favoring tradition, limited government, and established institutions

concepts.label: Anarchism
concepts.definition: Political philosophy advocating abolition of all forms of involuntary government

concepts.label: Fascism
concepts.definition: Far-right authoritarian ultranationalist political ideology

concepts.label: Libertarianism
concepts.definition: Political philosophy maximizing individual liberty and minimizing government

concepts.label: Civil Rights
concepts.definition: Rights of citizens to political and social freedom and equality

concepts.label: Freedom of Speech
concepts.definition: Right to express opinions without government restraint

concepts.label: Freedom of Religion
concepts.definition: Right to practice any religion or no religion without government interference

concepts.label: Separation of Church and State
concepts.definition: Principle of distance between religious and government institutions

concepts.label: Impeachment
concepts.definition: Process of charging a government official with misconduct while in office

concepts.label: Veto
concepts.definition: Constitutional right to reject a decision or proposal made by a legislature

concepts.label: Amendment
concepts.definition: Formal change or addition to a legal document, especially a constitution

concepts.label: Bill of Rights
concepts.definition: First ten amendments to the US Constitution guaranteeing fundamental rights

concepts.label: Automation
concepts.definition: Use of technology to perform tasks with reduced human intervention

concepts.label: GDPR
concepts.definition: EU General Data Protection Regulation protecting personal data and privacy

concepts.label: Adenosine
concepts.definition: Neurotransmitter promoting sleep and suppressing arousal

concepts.label: Meditation
concepts.definition: Practice of training attention and awareness for mental clarity and calm

concepts.label: Acoustics
concepts.definition: Branch of physics dealing with the study of mechanical waves in gases, liquids, and solids

concepts.label: Cultural Relativism
concepts.definition: Idea that a person's beliefs should be understood in the context of their own culture

concepts.label: Hinduism
concepts.definition: Oldest major world religion originating in the Indian subcontinent

concepts.label: Evolutionary Biology
concepts.definition: Study of evolutionary processes that produced diversity of life on Earth

concepts.label: Persuasion
concepts.definition: Process of influencing attitudes, beliefs, or behaviors through communication
